acceptance: cd cli && go build ./tools/lint/evidencelint/... passes. cd cli && go test ./tools/lint/evidencelint/... passes with test fixtures covering all four sink patterns (RunOptions.Prompt assignment, mcpContent Type:text construction, os.ReadFile result flowing into a *prompt* variable, server handler writing doc-sourced response bytes). Adversarial test: a test fixture introducing one unannotated violation per pattern makes the analyzer exit non-zero with a message that names the file and line. Positive test: a fixture with the same violation carrying an evidence:allow-unbounded comment passes the analyzer. lefthook run pre-commit on a synthetic branch that introduces an unannotated violation exits non-zero. Running the analyzer against the current tree (before Stages B–E migrate call sites) exits non-zero (confirms it would actually have caught the original problem).
description:
Stage A2 of FEAT-022. Implements the repo-wide lint rule enforcing
the no-unbounded-prompts invariant (FEAT-022 §3 + Non-Functional §Lint).
Satisfies US-226.

Depends on Stage A1 (ddx-64299657) — needs the evidence-package
primitives to exist so the lint rule can whitelist them.

Tooling choice (decided, no worker ambiguity): implement as a Go
analyzer under cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/ runnable via
`go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint ./...` and wired
into Lefthook pre-commit as an additional check. This is the simplest
path that works with the existing golangci-lint + go-vet stack and
avoids a custom golangci-lint plugin build. If this choice proves
wrong, revisit in a follow-up bead — do not expand scope here.

The analyzer flags, and requires either routing through evidence.* or
an `evidence:allow-unbounded reason="..."` comment on:
- Any assignment expression whose LHS is a selector matching
  `*.Prompt` where the receiver type is the agent RunOptions struct.
- Any composite-literal construction of `mcpContent` with `Type: "text"`.
- Any call to `os.ReadFile` / `ioutil.ReadFile` whose result flows
  into a variable named `*prompt*` or `*Prompt*` (structural heuristic
  covering the identified ingress sites).
- Any HTTP handler function writing response bytes that it obtained
  from library-doc, execution-bundle, diff, session, or persona
  sources (detected structurally by the source function's package
  prefix, not general dataflow).

Static/literal prompt fragments (constant string expressions compiled
into the binary) are exempt.

In-scope files:
- cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/ (new): analyzer.go, analyzer_test.go,
  and cmd/evidencelint/main.go entry point.
- lefthook.yml (existing): add evidence-lint pre-commit hook.
- .github/workflows/ CI config: add evidence-lint job or fold into
  existing lint job.

Out-of-scope:
- Any existing prompt-building call site. This bead only adds the
  analyzer. Existing violations are expected and tolerated via
  `evidence:allow-unbounded` annotations added in subsequent stages
  (B–E) as those stages migrate each site.
- Dataflow analysis. Structural coverage only — per FEAT-022
  Non-Functional §Lint.

Rollback: remove cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/, revert lefthook.yml
and CI workflow edits.
title: evidence: structural sink lint rule enforcing no-unbounded-prompts invariant (Stage A2)

description:
Bead 1 of 4 children under epic ddx-3562c48c. Depends on bead 0 (ddx-2d9026d0) for spec.

Extract a shared skill installer used by both ddx init and ddx install. Real-files-only; no symlinks. Wire ddx init to it.

NEW FILE: cli/internal/skills/install.go
```go
package skills
import "io/fs"
type Options struct { Force bool }
func Install(src fs.FS, projectRoot string, opts Options) error
```

Behavior:
- Copies skills from src into <projectRoot>/.agents/skills/<name>/ and <projectRoot>/.claude/skills/<name>/.
- Real files only. NEVER creates symlinks.
- Path traversal defense: rejects '..', absolute paths, symlink-escape outside projectRoot — return non-nil error before any write.
- Force semantics: pre-existing SYMLINK destinations removed UNCONDITIONALLY before write. Pre-existing REAL-FILE destinations skipped per-skill (whole dir) when Force=false; overwritten when Force=true.
- Tarball broken-symlink recovery: when source's .agents/skills/<skill> is broken symlink, fall back to <source>/skills/<skill>.
- embed.FS satisfies fs.FS (used by init for bootstrap ddx skill); plugin path uses os.DirFS(<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<plugin>) (covered in bead 2).
- .ddx/skills/ registration stays bootstrap-only — plugin skills do NOT get registered there.

- DELETE registerProjectSkills (cli/cmd/init.go:464+).

acceptance:
1. FEAT-006, FEAT-010, FEAT-001, DDx skill docs, and routing beads state the boundary: DDx chooses MinPower/MaxPower request bounds and retry escalation; agent owns harness/provider/model routing.
2. The active docs corpus has been scrubbed: active FEAT/TD/SD/test-plan/operator docs use run/try/work and MinPower/MaxPower consistently, and any remaining legacy command/routing references are explicitly allowlisted as historical/deprecation/migration context.
3. A checked-in docs grep allowlist/check fails new unallowlisted references to ddx agent run, ddx agent execute-bead, ddx agent execute-loop, --profile, --quorum, --harnesses, DDx-side ResolveRoute execution, profile ladders, or model overrides in active docs.
4. Normal ddx run/try/work execution paths never call ResolveRoute and never re-inject RouteDecision values into Execute.
5. ServiceExecuteRequest receives MinPower/MaxPower and a passthrough envelope on normal execution paths; run evidence records requested_min_power, requested_max_power, requested_passthrough, actual_model, and actual_power returned by the agent.
6. ddx work is no longer an alias for agent execute-loop; it may accept harness/provider/model only as passthrough envelope fields and exposes no DDx-owned routing semantics for them.
7. DDx retry policy uses named eligible/ineligible DDx attempt outcomes to choose higher MinPower on retry, including a top-model threshold derived from agent catalog power numbers; retry policy does not inspect or mutate passthrough constraint values.
8. Passthrough exhaustion stops with blocked_by_passthrough_constraint or agent_power_unsatisfied and never removes pins, widens pins, calls ResolveRoute, or loops escalation.
9. NewServiceFromWorkDir does not filter endpoint candidates by live /models responses or infer model-class routing policy; status surfaces may probe separately.
10. route-status/providers UI may consume ResolveRoute/Candidates or model/power catalog for observability; execution evidence records only requested power bounds, requested passthrough constraints, task facts, and actual route/cost/power returned by Execute.
11. routinglint or equivalent CI guard fails ResolveRoute, RoutePreflight, BuiltinCatalog profile mapping, endpoint live filtering, model-class routing policy, and raw Harness/Provider/Model reads outside the passthrough allowlist.
12. Historical default-route regression is covered: a clean project does not silently force openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini from DDx-side routing logic.
description:
## Goal

Simplify DDx agent execution so DDx does not participate in model/provider/harness routing while still owning bead retry policy.

DDx may:
- estimate work effort;
- request abstract agent power bounds using MinPower and optional MaxPower;
- accept operator passthrough constraints (`--harness`, `--provider`, `--model`) and send them unchanged to the agent;
- inspect the agent's available model/power catalog only to choose abstract MinPower thresholds, including top-model-only retries;
- decide whether a bead attempt succeeded using DDx-owned evidence: commits, merge/preserve result, no-changes rationale, post-run gates, review verdicts, cooldowns, and run metadata;
- retry a bead with higher MinPower when policy permits;
- pass non-routing execution intent to the agent: prompt/workdir, power bounds, effort/reasoning, permissions, timeouts, session/evidence metadata, passthrough constraints, and task facts such as EstimatedPromptTokens or RequiresTools.

The agent owns all routing inside the power bounds and passthrough constraints: harness/provider/model validation, endpoint choice, candidate scoring, health, quota, fallback, compatibility errors, and route errors. The agent reports actual model and actual power used, e.g. `running with qwen 3.6-27b (power 10)`.

## Non-goals

- No legacy compatibility paths for DDx-side ladders, model overrides, profiles, or default-path preflight routing.
- No DDx interpretation of harness/provider/model passthrough values.
- No DDx-owned provider probing/filtering in execution setup.
- No candidate traces in execution evidence from ResolveRoute.
- No cheap/standard/smart profile taxonomy in DDx execution policy.

## Required command layering

1. `ddx run`: one invocation atom. Calls agent Execute exactly once with requested MinPower/MaxPower and optional passthrough constraints. Does not call ResolveRoute.
2. `ddx try <bead>`: one bead attempt. Builds bead prompt/worktree/evidence context, estimates initial or retry MinPower, carries passthrough constraints unchanged, then calls run.
3. `ddx work`: queue drain and retry policy. Picks beads, invokes try, classifies results, and schedules higher-MinPower retries when policy permits. It does not inspect passthrough constraint values.

## Passthrough envelope rule

Harness/provider/model fields must live in one narrow agent-passthrough envelope. DDx may copy the envelope between layers and persist it for audit. DDx must not validate, branch on, fallback, rewrite, widen, or use these fields for queue/retry policy. If retry MinPower changes, the passthrough envelope remains unchanged and the agent resolves compatibility.

If hard pins make requested power bounds unsatisfiable, DDx stops with a typed terminal classification such as `blocked_by_passthrough_constraint` or `agent_power_unsatisfied`, records the agent evidence, and reports operator action required. DDx must not remove pins, choose alternatives, call ResolveRoute, or keep escalating.

## Retry eligibility

DDx may raise MinPower only for classified outcomes where stronger reasoning could plausibly help after a valid attempt started, such as capability-insufficient results, post-run check failures after an attempted change, review-blocked capability failures, or no-changes-after-attempt within the no-progress budget.

DDx must not use power retry for deterministic setup/operator-action failures: dirty worktree, merge or land conflict, missing checkout, invalid bead metadata, unresolved dependencies, config parse errors, missing harness binaries, auth failures, command-not-found/toolchain setup, passthrough exhaustion, or any case that would require inspecting/removing/replacing passthrough pins.

## Guardrails

Allowed raw passthrough touchpoints are CLI parsing, the single AgentPassthrough/AgentExecutionIntent value, Execute request construction, execution-record persistence, and human-facing evidence rendering. Retry/work/queue/catalog-threshold code carries the opaque value and may not read Harness, Provider, or Model fields. A routinglint/package-boundary check must enforce this allowlist.

Catalog/status rendering is observability only. Retry threshold policy may consume abstract catalog power numbers to compute MinPower thresholds. Status/catalog code must not feed concrete model/provider/harness choices into execution.

## Documentation corpus gate

This cleanup is not complete when only FEAT-006/FEAT-010 and the DDx skill are updated. The active documentation corpus must be scrubbed so operators, agents, and future implementation beads do not learn stale routing surfaces from older feature docs, test plans, technical designs, solution designs, or operator guides.

Active governing docs must describe `ddx run` / `ddx try` / `ddx work`, MinPower/MaxPower, passthrough-only pins, and status/debug-only ResolveRoute. Remaining references to `ddx agent run`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `ddx agent execute-loop`, quorum/profile flags, or DDx-side routing vocabulary must be either removed from active docs or explicitly allowlisted as historical/deprecation/migration context with a reason. A grep-based docs check must make this enforceable.

## Review findings incorporated

- ddx-da19756a: execution path reinjects informational RouteDecision.
- ddx-d4100b7e: execution request drops DDx-owned power intent and actual-power evidence.
- ddx-1997b22d: ddx work preserves legacy execute-loop routing surface.
- ddx-b6a2417b: FEAT-006/specs still bless DDx-side ladders and overrides.
- ddx-a65fea8a: DDx service adapter performs endpoint/model policy.
- ddx-0f42c934: route-status scope leaks candidate traces into execution evidence.
- ddx-d06ae28c: live symptom where default work routes to cheap/incorrect model.
- ddx-6036e1bc: status/catalog work must remain observability and power-threshold only.
- ddx-8e9a11c4: plan aligned to upstream MinPower/MaxPower contract.
- ddx-52ce6249: public docs/skills use run/try/work instead of legacy agent run/execute surfaces.
- ddx-6232daf5: retry eligibility matrix added.
- ddx-b74d4ac6: passthrough exhaustion stop semantics added.
- ddx-b0d96cb9: mechanical passthrough ownership guardrails required.
- ddx-8a617e91: catalog/status display separated from retry threshold policy.
- ddx-02335e0b: repo-wide active docs corpus scrub is a first-class gate.

## Execution order

1. Align specs/docs and test expectations to the MinPower/MaxPower boundary with passthrough constraints.
2. Scrub the active docs corpus and add the docs grep allowlist/check so stale legacy command/routing guidance cannot remain hidden outside the core feature docs.
3. Pull the upstream agent power-bounds interface release: Execute accepts MinPower/MaxPower and passthrough constraints, responses report actual model/power, and model catalog exposes available powers.
4. Remove ResolveRoute and route preflight from run/try/work execution paths.
5. Pass MinPower/MaxPower and passthrough envelope through ServiceExecuteRequest and persist requested bounds, actual power/model, and requested passthrough in run evidence.
6. Replace work alias/execute-loop surface with run <- try <- work layering.
7. Implement DDx retry power policy using the eligibility matrix and agent catalog power thresholds, without inspecting passthrough values.
8. Remove DDx endpoint/model policy from execution adapter.
9. Rescope route-status/provider UI candidate traces to status/debug only.
10. Add routinglint and regression tests that forbid ResolveRoute/model-policy code in execution paths while allowing passthrough envelope plumbing.
notes: Amendment incorporated: --harness/--provider/--model are allowed as passthrough-only agent constraints. They must live in a narrow envelope, remain sticky across retries, and never drive DDx routing/retry decisions.
title: epic: simplify DDx run/try/work to power-only agent execution

description:
Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go TestExecuteBeadHarnessNoiseNotSynthesized() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadHarnessNoiseNotSynthesized$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

Governing concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. Parent: ddx-d9df348d.

description:
Opus Stage 3 batch review (2026-04-15): Houdini 1.5.10 has unresolved peer-dep incompatibility with vite 8 + kit 2.57. STACK.md recommended Option A (downgrade vite) or Option B (drop Houdini for graphql-request). Stage 3.4 shipped with neither — works for now because the single NodeInfo query is trivial.

Stage 4.12 Workers page requires graphql-ws subscriptions (workerProgress). Houdini 1.5.10 subscription support on Vite 8 is the most likely place the peer-dep workaround falls apart.

Decision required BEFORE Stage 4.12: either (a) migrate to houdini@next (removes the 'upgrade to houdini@next' noise and fixes Vite 8 support), or (b) drop Houdini entirely for graphql-request + graphql-ws (lower abstraction, no codegen).

acceptance: (1) `ddx agent check` reports runtime liveness for every configured provider, not config sanity. (2) `ddx agent check --provider vidar` probes only vidar and reports result. (3) `ddx agent doctor` still exists and is about config/registry health, NOT provider liveness (semantic separation preserved). (4) Exit code 0 when at least one provider is reachable and has at least one model; 1 otherwise. (5) `go build ./...` passes. (6) Manual: stop lmstudio on vidar temporarily, run `ddx agent check --provider vidar`, see explicit unreachable status; restart, run again, see connected + model count.
description:
Add a new DDx CLI subcommand `ddx agent check [--provider X]`. Semantic distinction from existing `ddx agent doctor`: doctor answers "is my config failed?" (config validation, malformed YAML, missing API keys); check answers "what agents can I use right now?" (runtime liveness, which providers respond, which models are currently available). Both commands coexist.

Implementation lives in cli/cmd/agent_check.go. Mirrors ddx-agent's `cmdCheck` — probes each configured provider's /v1/models endpoint, reports reachability and current model inventory, exits 0 if at least one provider is reachable and has at least one usable model, exits 1 otherwise. Uses the Go package, not shell-out.
title: Visibility 5: ddx agent check [--provider X] — runtime availability

acceptance: All text-lever usages removed from .svelte files. Replaced with text-accent-lever dark:text-dark-accent-lever. The .text-lever definition in app.css removed or marked deprecated. Dark mode ID columns now correctly use dark-accent-lever.
description:
The CSS utility class .text-lever is defined in src/app.css line 190 as a legacy alias for --status-open-text (the open bead status color). It is used in at least 6 files as a shorthand for ID column coloring:

- src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/+page.svelte (lines 7, 13)
- src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/+page.svelte (line 207)
- src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/sessions/+page.svelte (line 367)
- src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/beads/+layout.svelte (line 438)
- src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/+layout.svelte (line 382)
- src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/[workerId]/+page.svelte (line 402)
- src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/beads/+page.svelte (line 247)

The intent is to style IDs with the accent-lever color. Using .text-lever instead of text-accent-lever dark:text-dark-accent-lever means the ID color does not adapt to dark mode (it's always the light-mode status-open-text color), and it obscures the semantic intent.

The .text-lever utility in app.css should be deprecated. All usages should be replaced with text-accent-lever dark:text-dark-accent-lever.
title: global: text-lever legacy CSS utility used in 5+ files — should be fully replaced by text-accent-lever

acceptance: After a `no_changes` execute-loop result where `base_rev == result_rev`, the bead persists `execute-loop-retry-after`, `execute-loop-last-status`, and `execute-loop-last-detail`; `ddx bead ready --json --execution` suppresses the bead until the retry-after time; and a deterministic test covers the tracker-backed path rather than only in-memory worker logic.
description:
<context-digest>
Follow-up observation from the HELIX alpha run that launched `helix-fef22846` via `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` and returned `status=no_changes`, `detail=agent made no commits`, `base_rev=result_rev=de67981a79a27ab8449bf6c475dda677fb25acfc`.
</context-digest>

`ddx agent execute-loop` reported a no-progress result but did not persist the retry-suppression metadata that `ReadyExecution()` expects, so the same bead remained at the front of the execution-ready queue immediately after the attempt.

## Reproduction
1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` and get a `no_changes` result where `base_rev == result_rev`.
2. Inspect bead `helix-fef22846` in `.ddx/beads.jsonl` or via `ddx bead show --json`.
3. Observe the new `execute-bead` event is present, but `execute-loop-retry-after`, `execute-loop-last-status`, and `execute-loop-last-detail` are absent.
4. Run `ddx bead ready --json --execution` again.
5. Observe `helix-fef22846` is still the first execution-ready candidate instead of being suppressed for the cooldown window.

## Observed gap
`ExecuteBeadWorker.Run()` calls `SetExecutionCooldown()` for no-progress outcomes, but the tracker-backed HELIX run did not retain any of the cooldown fields that `ReadyExecution()` checks.

## Why this matters
Without durable cooldown metadata, `execute-loop` can repeatedly burn agent time and tokens on the same no-op bead in back-to-back scans.
title: Persist execute-loop cooldown metadata after no-progress attempts

acceptance: 1. docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md updated; net length within +/- 30% of current 177 lines. 2. docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md updated; principles list reduced to load-bearing items only. 3. Three-layer architecture (DDx/HELIX/Dun) introduced in vision overview. 4. 'Platform vs workflow' boundary explicit in vision and principles. 5. The 6 settled decisions (project-local, cost-tiered, endpoint-first, single-skill, bead-driven, three-layer) are reflected — not necessarily by name, but the underlying ideas are present and consistent. 6. No filler, no marketing language, no aspirational features that haven't shipped. 7. Frontmatter and HELIX IDs preserved.
description:
V1 of 3 children under epic ddx-53c96967.

REFINE (do not rewrite) docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md and docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md to reflect what's settled.

CURRENT STATE:
- product-vision.md (177 lines) — written before several decisions landed. Has aspirational language and outdated references.
- principles.md (72 lines) — short list of guiding principles. Some are load-bearing, some are filler.
- prd.md (370 lines) and concerns.md (257 lines) are reference context — read for background, but DO NOT modify them in this bead.

DECISIONS THAT HAVE SETTLED AND SHOULD BE REFLECTED:
1. DDx is the platform layer; HELIX is the workflow layer; Dun is the quality layer. Three-project stack with explicit boundaries.
2. Project-local everything: ddx init/install only touch <projectRoot>. The only global ddx artifact is ddx-server. (FEAT-015, just shipped.)
3. Cost-tiered work: cheap models do, strong models review, deterministic fixes at the top of the ladder. Goal is closed-bead throughput per dollar.
4. Endpoint-first routing redesign: providers are endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match (in progress; reference don't promise).
5. Single-skill consolidation: DDx ships ONE 'ddx' skill (not seven). Per FEAT-011.
6. Bead-driven work: beads are the unit of work; execute-loop drains the queue with auto-recovery and review.

REFINEMENT GOALS:
- Sharpen: every sentence load-bearing. Cut filler.
- Prune principles to ones that drive actual decisions. If a principle isn't cited in any spec, remove it.
- Surface the three-layer architecture early in the vision.
- Make the 'platform vs workflow' boundary explicit.
- Voice: confident, technical, concise. No marketing fluff.

CONSTRAINT: in-place edits via Edit tool. Preserve sections that work. Do not full-rewrite. Do not change frontmatter/IDs.
title: refine vision + principles (in-place sharpening of frame docs)

acceptance:
1. New regression test cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestExecuteLoopRetryReusesWorkerHarness asserts: spawn execute-loop with --harness claude; bead reaches REQUEST_CHANGES → reopen → next iteration's service.Execute request has Harness="claude" (not empty).
2. Same test covers --model, --profile, and --provider propagation across review-driven reopens.
3. Test covers all reopen sources (review BLOCK, review REQUEST_CHANGES, land_conflict, post_run_check_failed, no_changes if it triggers reopen) — every reopen path must propagate pins.
4. Negative test: worker spawned without --harness; reopen retry continues to use auto-routing (no propagation regression).
5. cd cli && go test ./... -count=1 passes.
description:
Re-filed from agent-9465287e in /Users/erik/Projects/agent (closed there as 'tracked upstream'; the agent service is not the failure surface). Originally observed in ddx v0.6.2-alpha1 against agent v0.9.9 during SD-024 work.

## Symptom

Worker spawned with explicit pin:

    ddx work --harness claude

First attempt on a bead routes correctly to claude (e.g. opus-4.7) and produces a result. When the post-merge reviewer returns REQUEST_CHANGES the bead reopens. The next execute-loop iteration retries the bead but the worker's --harness override is NOT propagated to that retry. Tier-attempt events show:

    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=standard harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found
    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=smart    harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found
    kind:escalation-summary winning_tier=exhausted

Note harness= and model= empty — the retry constructs a service request with Harness="". Smart-routing returns zero candidates at every tier above cheap (cheap suppressed by adaptive min-tier from prior failures). Bead bounces between REQUEST_CHANGES (cycle 1) → execution_failed (cycle 2 with no harness routing) repeatedly.

Concrete instance: ddx-dd7fce7e in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/beads.jsonl shows the pattern at events around 2026-04-25T21:46-21:47Z.

## Principle

**Retries should use the same parameters as the initial work.** A reopened bead's retry must inherit every explicit pin (harness, model, model-ref, profile, provider) that the worker was spawned with. Otherwise the retry is a different request, not a retry — and the work cannot converge.

This is a general invariant, not specific to harness overrides. Any explicit pin the worker was launched with must propagate to every iteration on every bead the worker handles, including post-reopen retries.

## Diagnosis (from agent-9465287e worker analysis)

The agent service surface (CONTRACT-003) is correct. The agent has no tier concept — tier classification and adaptive min-tier floors are ddx-side abstractions layered on top of the agent's per-call routing. Loosening the agent's routing engine to return candidates when the harness is empty would change behavior for every other caller and is not the right lever.

The fix is a few lines of option-propagation in the ddx execute-loop's reopen-retry path: when a bead reopens after REQUEST_CHANGES (or any review-driven reopen), the loop must copy the worker-level explicit pins into the next iteration's ExecuteBeadOptions / service.Execute request, the same way it does on the initial claim.

## In-scope files

- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or wherever the retry path constructs its execute-bead invocation)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go if review-driven reopens have a separate path
- Regression test asserting the second service.Execute call carries the same Harness, Model, Profile values as the first when the bead was reopened by a reviewer

## Out of scope

- Agent-side routing engine changes — the surface is correct
- Adaptive min-tier behavior tuning (separate concern)
- Tool-loop detection (separate concern, see agent-1bdb9b60 in agent repo)

3. cd /home/erik/Projects/ddx && /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx work --once --local --escalate preserves the existing tier-loop behavior: at least one kind:tier-attempt event is emitted, ResolveProfileLadder is called (verifiable via agent.ResolveProfileLadderCallCount() in tests)
4. Unit test in cli/internal/server/workers_test.go asserts: with spec.Escalate=false, agent.ResolveProfileLadderCallCount() does not advance during executor invocation; with spec.Escalate=true, it advances
5. Operator pin preserved: cd cli && go test ./internal/server -run "TestWorker.*Harness|TestWorker.*Model" passes; --harness and --model flags continue to bypass escalation
6. docs/migrations/routing-config.md cross-references that the server worker path now matches the CLI path semantics (one-line addition or a brief paragraph)
description:
## Goal

Align `cli/internal/server/workers.go`'s default-path escalation semantics with the migration that already shipped on the CLI path via bead `ddx-87fb72c2` (epic `ddx-fdd3ea36`, see `docs/migrations/routing-config.md`). Today the two paths disagree:

- **CLI path** `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1792`: `escalationEnabled := escalate && harness == "" && model == ""` — escalation is opt-in via the `--escalate` flag.
- **Server path** `cli/internal/server/workers.go:650`: `escalationEnabled := spec.Harness == "" && spec.Model == ""` — escalation is implicitly on whenever neither harness nor model is pinned. No `--escalate` equivalent plumbed through the worker spec.

Because `ddx work` submits to the server worker by default (not `--local`), the user-facing surface still runs the tier loop + `ResolveTierCandidates` cooldown probe + `escalation.AdaptiveMinTier` filter on every dispatch — even though the migration declared the tier ladder opt-in. This is the proximate cause of the "all tiers exhausted — no viable provider" failure observed on `worker-20260429T033325-bedf` against this project today, and was confirmed by a control probe in this session: `ddx work --once --local` against `ddx-fdd3ea36` (no flags, no pin) reproduces "all tiers exhausted" while `ddx work --once --local --no-adaptive-min-tier` succeeds end-to-end on the cheap tier (qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b @ lmstudio-bragi, zero cost).

## Concrete change

Plumb an explicit escalation-opt-in flag through the worker spec, matching the CLI path's `--escalate` semantics:

1. Add a boolean field to `WorkerSpec` (in `cli/internal/server/workers.go` or wherever `WorkerSpec` is defined) named e.g. `Escalate`. Default `false`.
2. The `--escalate` flag on `ddx work` (already exists for the CLI path; verify it's plumbed to the worker submission, add the wiring if not) sets `WorkerSpec.Escalate = true`.
3. Change `cli/internal/server/workers.go:650` from:

       escalationEnabled := spec.Harness == "" && spec.Model == ""

   to:

       escalationEnabled := spec.Escalate && spec.Harness == "" && spec.Model == ""

4. Update `cli/internal/server/workers_test.go` to assert: with `spec.Escalate=false` (default), `executor` follows the `singleTierAttempt` path at line 692-702 — no `ResolveProfileLadder` call, no tier loop, no `AdaptiveMinTier` evaluation. With `spec.Escalate=true`, the existing tier-loop behavior is preserved.

That is all. Do not delete any helper functions in this bead — `ResolveProfileLadder`, `ResolveTierModelRef`, `AdaptiveMinTier` all remain (they're still consumed via the `--escalate` opt-in path). Their deletion is reserved for a separate cleanup bead after this lands and there's evidence no caller uses them.

## In-scope files

- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — line ~650, plus any `WorkerSpec` struct definition (could be in this file or `cli/internal/server/types.go` / `models.go` — locate via `grep -nE "type WorkerSpec\b" cli/`).
- `cli/internal/server/workers_test.go` — add the two assertions above.
- The `ddx work --escalate` flag wiring on the worker-submit path. If `--escalate` is currently CLI-only (i.e. only consulted in the `--local` branch), this bead also plumbs it through `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go`'s server-submit code path. Locate via `grep -nE "GetBool\\(\"escalate\"\\)|--escalate" cli/cmd/`.
- Possibly `cli/internal/server/server.go` if `WorkerSpec` is constructed there from an HTTP/MCP request body.

## Out of scope (do NOT touch)

- `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go` — keep all helpers; deletion is a separate bead.
- `cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go::AdaptiveMinTier` — keep; deletion is a separate bead.
- Default profile change from `default` to `local` — separate bead (`routing-unify-2`).
- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1792` — already correct; no change.
- `docs/migrations/routing-config.md` — already correct; describes the migration this bead completes for the server path.
- Any work on `cli/internal/agent` or `agent` SDK — agent already exposes everything DDx needs.

## Acceptance

1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/...` passes.
2. `cd /home/erik/Projects/ddx && /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx work --once --local` (no flags, no pin) against this project resolves and dispatches a single-call attempt. Verifiable by:
   - The bead receives at most one `kind:routing` event from the new flow (matches the `singleTierAttempt` pattern), not multiple `kind:tier-attempt skipped` events from the tier loop.
   - The execute-loop log line `adaptive min-tier: skipping cheap tier ...` does NOT appear.
3. `cd /home/erik/Projects/ddx && /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx work --once --local --escalate` against this project preserves the existing tier-loop behavior. Verifiable: at least one `kind:tier-attempt` event appears, the `adaptive min-tier` line still prints when applicable.
4. Unit test in `cli/internal/server/workers_test.go` asserts: with `spec.Escalate=false`, `agent.ResolveProfileLadder` is not called (use the existing call-counter test seam in `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go::ResolveProfileLadderCallCount`); with `spec.Escalate=true`, the call counter advances.
5. Operator policy preserved: `ddx work --harness X` and `ddx work --model Y` continue to work and bypass escalation regardless of `--escalate` (the existing `&& spec.Harness == "" && spec.Model == ""` predicate handles this).
6. The migration doc `docs/migrations/routing-config.md` is updated to mention that the worker path now matches the CLI path semantics, OR a one-line cross-reference is added if its current text is sufficient.

## Why this is a tight bead

It is a single 1-line behavioral change in `workers.go:650` plus the worker-spec field plumbing. The supervisor checkpoint mandate is "one bead/fix at a time"; this is the smallest meaningful step toward unification given the current tree state. Do not bundle helper deletions, default-profile changes, or schema cleanups — those are reserved for follow-up beads.

## Rollback

If the change breaks anything: revert the workers.go diff. The pre-change behavior was the prior implicit-on default; reverting restores it. Worker-spec field can stay (additive, default false). Migration doc edit is independent.
title: routing-unify-1: align workers.go default escalation semantics with the --escalate opt-in CLI path

acceptance: Either: (a) review step succeeds (rc=0, REVIEW_STATUS trailer present, structured verdict captured by harness) on a fresh helix run cycle against any repo with --agent claude, OR (b) the review-trailer requirement is removed from the harness and salvage becomes the explicit primary path with appropriate logging. Whichever decision is made, the operator-facing summary lines should not show rc=1 for an outcome that the harness considers normal.
description:
During a 12-cycle 'helix run --agent claude --summary' against the heimq repo on 2026-04-26, every single post-implementation review step returned rc=1 with 0 tokens and the harness logged 'review output missing REVIEW_STATUS trailer'. The harness salvaged each cycle because the tracker showed progress (closed N → N+1), but this means:

1. The structured REVIEW_STATUS verdict is never captured — we pay for the review cycle but lose its decision (block / approve / approve-with-followups).
2. The salvage path masks the fact that reviews are not actually gating anything.
3. Operators reading summary lines see rc=1 'failures' that are actually invisible reviews — confusing signal.

Sample log lines from /Users/erik/Projects/heimq/.helix-logs/helix-20260426-144028.log (run launched 14:40 UTC, completed 16:28 UTC):

  --- ddx agent run [14:51:08] harness=claude rc=0 exit=1 elapsed=350s cost=/bin/zsh tokens=0 ---
  --- end ddx agent output ---
  helix: review output missing REVIEW_STATUS trailer
  helix: review output was not safely interpretable after cycle 1 — continuing

This pattern repeated for every single one of the 12 cycles (same 0-tokens, same 'review output missing REVIEW_STATUS trailer' message after each cycle's review step).

Hypotheses to investigate:
- Review prompt template emitted to Claude does not actually instruct the agent to print a REVIEW_STATUS trailer in a parseable form.
- Prompt does instruct, but the trailer format the harness expects has drifted from what the prompt asks for.
- Claude's output is being truncated before the trailer (timeouts? token caps?).
- Review agent invocation is silently failing very early (0 tokens reported suggests it never got far) — could be a misconfigured CLI invocation, missing flag, env var.

Investigation steps:
1. Inspect what prompt/system message the helix harness sends to claude for the review step — confirm it asks for a REVIEW_STATUS line.
2. Inspect what the harness regexes for to detect the trailer.
3. Run one review step manually with verbose logging to capture full agent stdout.
4. Compare with codex agent — does the trailer issue exist there too, or is it claude-specific?

Why this matters: helix-run's correctness model assumes reviews actually gate progression. Right now reviews are decorative.
title: helix run: review agent rc=1 every cycle — REVIEW_STATUS trailer missing

description:
Stage B of FEAT-022. Collapse the duplicate review-prompt assembler in
cli/cmd/bead_review.go into agent.BuildReviewPrompt. A byte-equivalence
golden fixture, CAPTURED BEFORE THE DUPLICATE IS DELETED, gates the
removal.

Implements FEAT-022 requirement §4. Satisfies US-227.

Depends on Stage A1 (ddx-64299657) — evidence primitives must exist so
the golden-fixture test can import them cleanly (though this bead does
NOT yet change behavior via caps — that's Stage C1).

Workflow (non-negotiable, prevents half-done landings):

1. BEFORE touching any code, run the current (pre-collapse) CLI review
   path against a committed fixture bead/rev/refs and write the
   resulting prompt bytes to a golden file at
   cli/cmd/testdata/bead_review/collapse_golden.txt. Commit that
   golden as the first commit on this bead's branch. The fixture MUST
   include `<`, `>`, `&`, `\"`, and `'` characters in bead notes and
   governing-doc content so the escape-semantics divergence
   (xml.EscapeText in cmd vs NewReplacer in agent) is forced to
   surface.

2. Now collapse: delete cli/cmd/bead_review.go's buildBeadReviewPrompt
   (at :150), beadReviewInstructions const (at :43), and
   beadReviewXMLEscape (at :240). Rewire the CLI handler to call
   agent.BuildReviewPrompt directly.

3. If escape semantics differ between old and new on the committed
   fixture: converge on agent.reviewXMLEscape's behavior unless a
   specific failure mode requires the CLI's quote-escape. In that
   case extend BuildReviewPrompt with a typed flag parameter (e.g.
   EscapeQuotes bool). Do NOT keep a duplicate escape helper.

4. The post-collapse CLI review path MUST produce bytes identical to
   the golden file when run against the same fixture inputs.

In-scope files:
- cli/cmd/bead_review.go (deletions + rewire to agent package)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (only if a typed flag is
  needed per step 3; document and justify any addition)
- cli/cmd/testdata/bead_review/ (new golden fixture)
- cli/cmd/bead_review_test.go (new): golden-file diff test +
  CLI-handler wiring test

Out-of-scope (do NOT touch):
- cli/cmd/bead_review.go's beadReviewGitShow helper at :164 — this is
  git invocation, not prompt assembly. KEEP IT.
- Adding any cap / evidence-primitive usage to BuildReviewPrompt's
  assembly logic — that's Stage C1. This bead is pure deduplication.

Rollback: revert the deletions, restore duplicate symbols. Golden
fixture can be retained regardless.
title: review: collapse cmd/bead_review.go into agent.BuildReviewPrompt

acceptance: 1) TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorNoCommits no longer references fakeExecuteBeadGit or fakeAgentRunner. 2) Test dispatches through RealLandingGitOps + newScriptHarnessRepo (or equivalent real-git harness). 3) cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorNoCommits$' -count=1 green. 4) No other test in agent_execute_bead_test.go is modified.
description:
Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorNoCommits() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorNoCommits$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

Governing concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. Parent: ddx-d9df348d.

acceptance: FEAT-006, FEAT-012, FEAT-014, FEAT-015, and API-001 describe the same contract: execute-bead compiles a deterministic prompt from bead data plus resolved references; each attempt produces tracked machine-readable execution artifacts; ignored runtime scratch is clearly separated from tracked evidence; and commit-message metadata is projected from tracked files rather than ad hoc runtime state
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: governing contract for execute-bead prompts and execution evidence. Evidence covers the current FEAT-006/API-001 workflow, the prompt fallback bug, the desire to keep beads authored and relatively stable, and the new requirement that execution evidence be tracked in git so autoresearch can replay, compare, and analyze runs.
</context-digest>
Align the governing docs around one execute-bead evidence contract.

## Goals
- Define the execute-bead prompt template as a DDx-owned rationalizer over bead fields plus resolved governing references, not a bead mutation and not a speculative summary of whole specs
- Define the stable tracked artifact set for each execution attempt: prompt, manifest, result, checks, normalized log, and usage/provider pointers
- Define the split between tracked execution evidence and ignored local scratch/runtime state
- Define the default commit policy for successful and preserved attempts
- Define the rule that all programmatically-added commit message metadata must be rendered from tracked machine-readable files
title: Align specs for prompt templates, tracked execution artifacts, and commit provenance

acceptance: FEAT-006 and the execution-evidence design docs define embedded-agent runtime paths for managed execute-bead runs; the embedded harness no longer writes default root-level agent state inside execution worktrees; and tests prove session/telemetry output is redirected into DDx-owned evidence or runtime directories
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: embedded ddx-agent runtime ownership inside execute-bead worktrees. Evidence covers the tracked execution-evidence lane, the current rule that DDx should own execution artifacts for managed runs, and the need to prevent embedded-agent session/telemetry files from appearing as ad hoc root-level state inside execution worktrees.
</context-digest>
Define and implement the path contract for embedded ddx-agent logs and telemetry when DDx runs the embedded harness inside execute-bead and execute-loop.

## Goals
- Prevent embedded ddx-agent session logs, traces, or telemetry from being written as default root-level runtime state in managed execution worktrees
- Redirect embedded-agent runtime output into DDx-owned execution-evidence or runtime paths
- Keep the split explicit between tracked execution evidence and ignored local runtime scratch
- Update the governing specs so embedded-agent log ownership is documented rather than implied by implementation

## Required spec work
- Update FEAT-006 and the execution-evidence design lane to define where embedded-agent logs/telemetry live during execute-bead runs
- Clarify how embedded-agent-owned logs differ from external provider-native logs

## Required implementation work
- Configure the embedded agent harness so managed execute-bead worktrees do not accumulate root-level agent state
- Add tests proving embedded-agent runtime output is redirected into DDx-owned paths
title: Redirect embedded agent logs out of execute-bead worktree root

acceptance:
1. `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:e2e` exits 0.
2. The same 196 tests run as today (or more — net non-decreasing). No test is deleted, `.skip`-ed, or commented out to make the suite pass. Tolerated changes: rewriting test bodies to use the new node/project router; adding `beforeAll` setup that seeds or boots the harness.
3. `playwright.config.ts` spins up the Go ddx server (built binary or `go run`) on a deterministic port with a self-contained fixture, instead of `bun run preview`. Verifiable: `grep -E 'preview|webServer' playwright.config.ts` no longer mentions `preview` for the default project, and the new `webServer.command` invokes `ddx`/`go run`.
4. The fixture is fully under `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/`. Verifiable: `grep -rE '\$HOME|~/' e2e/fixtures/` returns nothing; the test run sets the server's working dir to a temp copy of the fixture, not the developer's actual workspace.
5. `playwright.demo.config.ts` is unchanged. Verifiable: `git diff --stat origin/main -- cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.demo.config.ts` shows 0 lines changed.
6. Runbook updated. `cli/internal/server/frontend/README.md` (or the project-canonical doc) describes the new prerequisite (binary build) and how to run the suite locally.
7. The 149 tests that pass today still pass. Verifiable: a clean checkout at the merge commit, run `bun run test:e2e`, exit 0.
description:
## Problem

`bun run test:e2e` from `cli/internal/server/frontend` exits 1: 42 of 196 tests fail, 149 pass, 5 skipped. The failures are not regressions — they are accumulated drift between the test suite and the current UI. `playwright.config.ts` runs only `bun run preview` (port 4173, static SvelteKit output, no Go backend), and the suite outgrew that scope.

Bead ddx-955b9c41 (closed 2026-04-21) flagged the split between `playwright.config.ts` (4173) and `playwright.demo.config.ts` (18080) as "likely intentional but undocumented". This epic resolves it.

## Failure breakdown

**(A) TC-008 HTTP API — 6 tests.** `e2e/app.spec.ts:368-409`. Direct `request.get('/api/...')` to `/api/health`, `/api/documents`, `/api/beads`, `/api/beads/status`, `/api/personas`, `/api/docs/graph`. Static preview returns 404; the Go server returns 200 for all six (verified with curl against the running ddx-server on https://127.0.0.1:7743 during the session that filed this bead).

**(B) TC-001 Dashboard — 8 tests.** `e2e/app.spec.ts:9-57`. All visit `/` and expect `h1` "Dashboard". Per Stage 3.8 (commit 3bf655cf), `src/routes/+page.svelte` does `goto(`/nodes/${nodeStore.value.id}`, { replaceState: true })` whenever a node is selected — `/` is no longer a Dashboard route. Tests were not migrated.

**(C) Pages requiring backend data — ~28 tests.** Spread across `e2e/app.spec.ts` (TC-002, TC-003, TC-005, TC-007), `e2e/beads-smoke.spec.ts`, `e2e/executions.spec.ts`, `e2e/navigation.spec.ts` (TC-006, TC-007), `e2e/screenshots.spec.ts`, `e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts`, `e2e/workers.spec.ts`, `src/routes/layout.e2e.ts`. All fail because the static preview can't serve real document/bead/session data.

## In-scope files

Configuration:
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.config.ts` — switch `webServer` to spawn the Go ddx server (built binary or `go run`) on a deterministic port with a fixture working dir, instead of `bun run preview`. Set `use.baseURL` to that port. Keep `bun run preview` available as a separate project ("static-only") for any tests that genuinely don't need a backend.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.demo.config.ts` — **leave alone**. Used for demo recording; do not modify.

Fixture:
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/<name>/` — new directory containing a seeded `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (handful of beads in varied states: open, closed, ready, blocked), a tiny `docs/` library (a few documents the API can index), and any minimum config the server needs. Self-contained — must NOT depend on the developer's `~/.config/ddx` or the parent repo's `.ddx/`.

Tests (categorize and migrate):
- `e2e/app.spec.ts` (TC-001, TC-002, TC-003, TC-005, TC-007, TC-008) — rewrite navigation paths to use `/nodes/<fixture-node-id>/projects/<fixture-project-id>/...`, matching the pattern in `e2e/navigation.spec.ts:177`. Drop assumptions about `/` resolving to a Dashboard route.
- `e2e/beads-smoke.spec.ts`, `e2e/executions.spec.ts`, `e2e/navigation.spec.ts`, `e2e/screenshots.spec.ts`, `e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts`, `e2e/workers.spec.ts`, `src/routes/layout.e2e.ts` — verify each passes against the new harness; update navigation paths and any data-shape assumptions where needed.

CI / runbook:
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/README.md` (or whatever is canonical) — document that `bun run test:e2e` now requires the ddx Go binary built first, and how the fixture is structured.

## Out-of-scope (file separate beads if needed)

- Server source changes in `cli/internal/server/` — the API is correct; this bead is about test wiring.
- Frontend route additions or component changes — only test files and fixtures change.
- `playwright.demo.config.ts` and `demo-recording.spec.ts` semantics — leave untouched (recording is its own concern).
- Visual regression baselines in `e2e/screenshots.spec.ts` — re-baselining if pixel diffs appear is a follow-up bead, not this one.

## Why an epic, not a single task

Per beads.md, anything that touches more than ~3 files or crosses subsystems should decompose. This crosses test-infra (playwright config + fixture seeding) and four distinct test files at minimum. Codex should decompose during refinement: at minimum, "(1) harness + fixture", "(2) migrate TC-001/TC-008", "(3) migrate page-data tests", and "(4) update runbook".

## Rollback / cleanup

If a child bead fails partway:
- Do not leave the fixture dir half-populated under `e2e/fixtures/`. Either commit a complete fixture or revert.
- Do not commit a `playwright.config.ts` that runs but skips tests (no `.skip` to make CI green).
- The currently-passing 149 tests must continue to pass at every checkpoint.

## Verification snapshot at filing time

- Working tree clean before run: confirmed.
- 196 tests collected, 149 pass, 42 fail, 5 skipped.
- Recent commits on main: d0945dc3 (frontend-verify Makefile target — unrelated to e2e), 5569a727 (beads.jsonl conflict resolve — unrelated).
- API endpoints verified live on https://127.0.0.1:7743: /api/health, /api/personas, /api/docs/graph, /api/beads, /api/documents all return 200.
notes:
Harness now builds frontend assets so most specs pass (176/189), but `bun run test:e2e` still exits non-zero with 13 failures, contradicting AC #1 (`exits 0`). Author claims remaining failures (screenshots/a11y/demo) are out-of-scope follow-ups, but AC #1 is unconditional.

Suite now exits 0 (AC #1 met), but two concerns: (a) 3 .svelte source files modified for color-contrast despite bead's explicit 'frontend route additions or component changes' out-of-scope rule; (b) retries:2 added to mask a pre-existing flake rather than fix it.
title: test-infra: e2e suite drifted from node/project router; needs Go-server harness + seeded fixture

acceptance:
1. graphRepairIssue mutation in schema; resolver atomically applies structured edit and re-validates.
2. IntegrityPanel shows 'Preview fix' for REMOVE_MISSING_DEP and APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID issues.
3. Preview expands inline showing frontmatter diff.
4. Clicking 'Apply fix' calls mutation; on success issue disappears and graph refreshes.
5. File-changed error: inline error message in issue row; copy-snippet fallback unchanged.
6. Ambiguous or non-auto-repairable issues: no 'Preview fix' shown; copy-snippet only.
7. Go resolver test covers: successful repair, stale file hash rejection, path traversal rejection.
description:
Add in-place repair actions to IntegrityPanel for auto-repairable integrity issues.

Backend mutation:
- graphRepairIssue(issueID: ID!, strategy: RepairStrategy!): GraphRepairResult
- RepairStrategy enum: REMOVE_MISSING_DEP, APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID, CLEAN_PATH_MAP
- GraphRepairResult: { success: Boolean!, newIssues: [GraphIssue!]!, error: String }
- Resolver: validates path is within project root, checks current file hash matches issue snapshot, applies structured YAML/frontmatter edit atomically, re-runs graph validation, returns updated issue list.
- NOT auto-repairable server-side: parse errors, cycles, ambiguous duplicates where both files have inbound references, missing required roots.

Auto-repairable strategies:
- REMOVE_MISSING_DEP: removes missing ID from depends_on array in frontmatter
- APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID: renames duplicate ID to the SHA-1 suffix suggestion (only for the one file that has no inbound references)
- CLEAN_PATH_MAP: updates stale path metadata in id_to_path map

UI changes to IntegrityPanel:
- Each auto-repairable issue shows 'Preview fix' button (in addition to existing copy-snippet)
- Preview expands to show a before/after diff of the frontmatter change
- 'Apply fix' button calls the mutation with confirmation (no additional modal for single-file fixes)
- On success: issue removed from panel; graph data refreshes; success toast
- On error (stale file, write failure, new issue introduced): inline error in issue row; no partial success hidden; copy-snippet still available as manual fallback

Failure modes handled: file changed since issue computed, YAML parse failure, permission/path traversal, repair introduces new graph issue, generated file (show warning 'this file may be auto-generated'), concurrent repair (server-side file hash check).
title: graph integrity repair: one-click fix for missing deps and duplicate IDs

acceptance: The governing docs and queue contain a coherent lane for single-ticket-first queue policy, epic-scoped workers, epic branch/worktree naming, child-bead sequencing, and regular merge-commit landing with epic merge gates
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: epic execution semantics after the execute-loop dogfood pass. Evidence covers the need to prioritize single tickets ahead of epics, keep open epics out of the ordinary execute-loop launch set, execute one epic sequentially in one persistent worktree, preserve child-ticket history on the epic branch, and land completed epics with a regular merge commit after epic-level merge gates pass.
</context-digest>
Define epic-scoped execution and merge behavior across DDx queueing, git, server, and UI surfaces.

## Goals
- Define single-ticket-first queue ordering with open epics excluded from the ordinary execute-loop worker
- Define one persistent branch/worktree per active epic and sequential child execution inside that worktree
- Define how child tickets close individually while their commits accumulate on the epic branch
- Define epic-level merge gates and regular merge-commit landing semantics
- Define the server/UI worker and queue surfaces needed to supervise epic workers alongside ordinary workers
title: Design epic-scoped execution and merge workflow

description:
## Context

Tracker bead for propagation of upstream ddx-agent fixes filed 2026-04-17/18.
Both fresh-eyes reviews (codex + opus, 2026-04-18) flagged the need for a
single consolidated tracker on the DDx side — this is it.

## Status table (refreshed 2026-04-19; DDx is now on agent v0.5.0)

| Upstream bead | Title                                 | Upstream status | DDx bump     | DDx commit  | DDx consumer / smoke                                                                                                       |
|---------------|---------------------------------------|-----------------|--------------|-------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| agent-92f0f324| DetectedFlavor                        | landed v0.3.12  | v0.3.11→12   | c9368312    | Covered by ddx-4817edfd (capability-gating epic; decomposed into 4 sub-beads, all closed).                                 |
| agent-767549c7| capability flags (SupportsTools)      | landed v0.3.12  | v0.3.11→12   | c9368312    | ddx-4817edfd sub-beads: context-window gating, tool-calling gating, effort/permissions gating, structured-output gating.   |
| agent-941e7e42| AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE                      | landed v0.3.12  | v0.3.11→12   | c9368312    | Used ad-hoc via scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh (regenerates fixtures); no CI-level regression test drives it.             |
| agent-04639431| strip thinking field on non-Anthropic | landed v0.3.13  | v0.3.12→13   | 9d057f83    | NO DDx CONSUMER NEEDED — the filter is entirely internal to agent's provider layer; DDx just sees the clean content stream.|
| agent-f237e07b| SSE comment-frame filter              | landed v0.3.14  | v0.3.13→14   | b59ebacf    | ddx-bbb2d177 smoke test landed in 1cd8e3a3 and was deleted in d5ebab2b during the v0.5.0 cutover (live-smoke equivalent now lives in the ddx-agent comparison/benchmark suite). Fixture files remain orphaned at cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/ and should be cleaned up in a follow-up. |
| agent-a365bcf2| preset rename (cheap/default/smart)   | in_progress     | —            | —           | Blocks this bead's closure. When it lands, file a DDx migration bead for any --preset references in .ddx/config.yaml (currently at least one harness uses '--preset codex').|

## v0.5.0 cutover context (2026-04-19)

Commit d5ebab2b cut DDx over to agent v0.5.0 and dropped the local replace
directive. That post-dates the v0.3.x-era fixes this tracker was originally
scoped around. v0.5.0 contracted the public surface (provider/openai now
internal/), which is why the omlx smoke test was removed rather than
migrated. Any residual v0.3.x consumer test would be orphaned by the surface
contraction anyway — the DDx side of that consumer coverage now lives in the
upstream agent repo's benchmark suite, and DDx's own execute-bead path is
covered by the broader test set in cli/internal/agent/.

## Remaining work before this bead closes

1. Upstream agent-a365bcf2 (preset rename) lands.
2. File a DDx-side migration bead: sweep .ddx/config.yaml, library templates,
   and any hardcoded preset strings for claude/codex/cursor/agent aliases;
   swap to smart/cheap/default/minimal; optionally keep a compatibility shim
   for one release.
3. Optional (P4): delete the orphaned cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/
   fixtures (no test consumes them post-v0.5.0) OR relocate them to the
   upstream benchmark suite.
4. Close this tracker bead.

## Non-goals

- Re-implementing the upstream fixes. They've landed.
- Tracking ddx-agent's own backlog (agent-b1c2d3e4 manifest v4 etc.). That's upstream's concern.
- Retrofitting v0.3.x-era consumer tests after the v0.5.0 surface contraction.
title: chore: track closure of upstream agent fixes on DDx side (thinking-strip, SSE-filter, preset rename)

acceptance: The queue contains a concrete contract-and-build lane for execute-loop no-change disposition, satisfaction-based auto-close, and retry suppression so queue burning does not stall on one unresolved no-op bead
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: execute-loop queue-drain reliability after the no-change misclassification fix. Evidence covers repeated installed-binary `execute-loop --once` runs selecting the same execution-ready bead, the new `no_changes` status, the need to auto-close already-satisfied beads without reintroducing false-success closure, and the requirement to avoid infinite retry loops on unresolved no-progress work.
</context-digest>
Improve execute-loop so repeated no-progress attempts are classified and handled in a way that lets the queue make forward progress without sacrificing correctness.

## Goals
- Distinguish already-satisfied no-change beads from unresolved no-progress attempts
- Prevent immediate re-execution loops on beads that repeatedly return `no_changes`
- Keep closure semantics machine-owned and evidence-backed rather than inferred from freeform agent output
- Preserve queue ordering semantics while making the queue drain behavior operationally useful
title: Improve execute-loop no-progress handling and queue-drain behavior

acceptance: Server and UI agent-activity surfaces reflect DDx invocation metadata plus native session/trace references, without promising full external transcript browsing

Review area: server and UI activity surfaces. Evidence covers FEAT-002, FEAT-008, and the new TD-006 ownership model for external vs embedded agent detail.
</context-digest>
Align DDx server and UI surfaces with the new invocation-activity model.

## Scope
- Update API/detail semantics for agent activity
- Surface native session and trace references where available
- Keep embedded ddx-agent detail richer when DDx owns or can reference it
- Avoid promising full external provider transcript browsing
title: Align server and UI agent-activity surfaces with native provider ownership

description:
Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

Governing concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. Parent: ddx-d9df348d.

description:
During investigation of ddx-7eab13a6 (bead-id resolver worktree bug), a design question surfaced: is it correct for execute-bead workers to call `ddx bead create` directly in-band?

Three alternatives:

(a) Append to parent bead. Worker appends discovered sub-tasks as structured items in the parent's notes or a new discovered-subtask event. The parent becomes the durable record; future runs or the operator can decompose.

(b) Surface via result. Agent emits a structured discovered_subtasks: [...] field in result.json; the execute-loop / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.

(c) Create beads in-band (current behavior). Worker calls ddx bead create directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue on over-decomposition.

Today's behavior is (c) but undocumented. One worker spawned 11 read-coverage children in a single run (ddx-44236615), all P0, without operator review. The work may be valid but the pattern needs a documented position.

Additionally, 22 existing beads have malformed IDs of the form .execute-bead-wt-<parent>-<timestamp>-<random>-<hex> (caused by the layer-1 bug fixed in ddx-7eab13a6). A mass-repair or close+refile decision should be made once the design position is set.

Scope: pick (a), (b), or (c) and document in a spec amendment (FEAT-006 or FEAT-010 if applicable); record the rationale. Also decide whether to repair the 22 existing malformed-ID beads.
title: layer-2 design: should execute-bead workers create beads in-band?

File: cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:159
Category: correctness
Severity: critical
Description: executeOnService currently does not carry DDx-owned execution intent correctly. With the updated agent interface, DDx must pass MinPower/MaxPower and opaque passthrough constraints to Execute, then record the agent's actual model/power response. Without that, DDx cannot own bead retry escalation or audit operator constraints.
Suggested fix: Populate ServiceExecuteRequest.MinPower/MaxPower and Harness/Provider/Model from the narrow passthrough envelope, capture actual model/power from Execute response/events, and persist requested_min_power/requested_max_power, actual model/power, plus requested passthrough in run and attempt evidence.

acceptance: DDx updates its ddx-agent dependency/reference to upstream v0.1.1; any required integration or config changes for retry ownership, telemetry, usage/session continuity, or tool path behavior are implemented; affected tests pass; and any noteworthy compatibility or rollout notes are recorded in the bead or linked docs
description:
Upstream `DocumentDrivenDX/agent` now has point release `v0.1.1`. Update DDx to consume that release and reconcile any integration assumptions that changed since `v0.1.0`.

Key upstream changes since `v0.1.0`:
- OTel telemetry landed for session, chat-attempt, and tool spans/metrics, with a documented telemetry contract and runtime-specific cost attribution.
- Provider retries moved into the runtime so providers execute one attempt per call and expose richer attempt metadata.
- Session continuity/history support landed in `Run`.
- `ddx-agent usage` reporting landed.
- Tool path handling now resolves symlink chains.
- Compaction behavior was hardened: prefix-aware accounting, balanced compaction events, and fail-closed handling when compaction cannot fit within budget.
- Self-update cache writing was fixed for first-run homes without an existing cache directory.
- Mixed-provenance known-cost session rollups now preserve the root known cost instead of downgrading to unknown.

DDx should review whether any wrapper logic, telemetry ingestion, usage/reporting integration, or runtime assumptions need adjustment when moving from `v0.1.0` to `v0.1.1`.
notes: Bumped github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent to v0.1.1 in cli/go.mod; no DDx wrapper/source changes were required because the v0.1.1 API remained compatible. Verified with 'go test ./internal/agent ./cmd -count=1' in the cli module.
title: Update DDx to ddx-agent v0.1.1

description:
## Scope
Add a context budget option that shrinks the execute-bead prompt for cheap-tier attempts by omitting large governing documents and including only the bead's own description, acceptance criteria, and the most relevant file content.

## Context
Local models (qwen3-coder-next, 32k context) frequently hit compaction limits on beads with large prompts. Shrinking the prompt for cheap-tier attempts lets them attempt more beads before escalating.

## Files to touch
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — add ContextBudget to ExecuteBeadOptions, gate prompt assembly
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go — add --context-budget flag
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_test.go — test minimal budget produces shorter prompt
title: Prompt-context pre-filter for cheap-tier attempts

description:
Review finding amended by passthrough and upstream MinPower/MaxPower requirement.
File: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:373
Category: correctness
Severity: critical
Description: DDx calls agent.ResolveRoute in the execution path, copies the informational RouteDecision into Harness/Provider/Model overrides, and then calls Execute. This violates the boundary. Under the updated interface, DDx may request MinPower/MaxPower bounds and may pass harness/provider/model operator constraints through unchanged, but must not pre-resolve or re-inject a concrete route.
Suggested fix: Remove ResolveRoute from run/try/work execution paths. Pass only caller intent/task facts to Execute: MinPower/MaxPower, opaque AgentPassthrough constraints, Reasoning/Effort, Permissions, EstimatedPromptTokens, RequiresTools, workdir/timeouts/metadata. Keep ResolveRoute only in status/debug surfaces.
title: bug: execution path reinjects informational route decisions

acceptance:
- [ ] execute-loop with zero flags escalates across cheap → standard → smart on failure
- [ ] Review agent auto-selects a tier >= impl tier, defaulting to smart
- [ ] Provider health probe runs at attempt time; unhealthy providers skip cleanly with cooldown
- [ ] Each attempt records tier + provider + probe_result + outcome on the bead
- [ ] Integration test: cheap provider 502 + standard succeeds → bead closes with escalation trail visible
- [ ] --min-tier / --max-tier overrides work and are documented
- [ ] --harness / --model debug overrides disable escalation
- [ ] Zero-flag 'ddx agent execute-loop' is the documented happy path
description:
The CLI should not require the user to enumerate harness/model pairs. The tool already knows which tiers exist (model-catalog.yaml: cheap/standard/smart) and which harnesses are configured. Given a live probe of each provider's health at attempt time, the loop can auto-escalate on its own.

## Principle

Discovery over configuration. The user shouldn't write a fallback chain — the tool should figure it out.

## Default policy (no flags)

- Start at **cheap** tier, execute the bead
- If merge succeeds AND post-review (gap #1) APPROVES → close
- If anything fails (execution_failed, no_changes after N retries, post_run_check_failed, REQUEST_CHANGES from review) → escalate one tier and retry
- Chain: cheap → standard → smart → exhausted (preserve, flag for human)
- Review agent runs at max(impl_tier+1, smart). Never review cheap with cheap.

## Resource discovery at attempt time

- Before picking a concrete harness/model for a tier, probe each candidate provider (local inference host, API key validity, network reachability)
- If provider returns 502 / 500 / timeout / auth-rejected within a short budget, mark it unhealthy with a cooldown and try the next provider in the tier
- If every provider in a tier is unhealthy, skip the tier and escalate
- Per-provider health state is process-local and visible via a new 'ddx agent providers' sub-command (ddx-09ea1884 Visibility 3)

## Rare overrides

- --min-tier standard (skip cheap — useful for hard beads known to need it)
- --max-tier standard (never reach smart — cost control)
- --harness <name> / --model <name> (debug override; disables escalation)

## Surface

Default invocation becomes:

  ddx agent execute-loop --once

Everything else is implicit. Review, escalation, and resource discovery are on-by-default.

## Events

Each attempt appends an execute-bead event: { harness, model, tier, provider, probe_result, attempt_status, review_status }. The event stream shows exactly why a tier/provider was picked or skipped.

## Context

Observed during axon ADR-018 loop:
- bragi was down (502) — I manually probed with curl and switched to vidar-coder. Tool should do this.
- qwen3-coder-next merged but opus review rejected — I manually re-invoked with sonnet. Tool should escalate automatically.
- Resulting CLI invocation in my notes was too complex (—-fallback-chain a/b,c/d,e/f --post-review g/h). User feedback: too many knobs. Reach for a default that just works.

## Acceptance criteria replaced with discovery-driven ones (see accept field)
notes:
<operator-feedback 2026-04-15>
Additional observation: --local is too prominent as a flag. The default IS server-worker mode, but I (an experienced DDx operator) still reached for --local reflexively because I wanted a synchronous handle to the subprocess. The root cause: I didn't know how to tail a server-side worker from the CLI.

Possible fixes (minor, could be part of this bead's scope):
- 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --follow' that submits to server and tails the worker log to stdout until completion, then returns. Gives the synchronous-feel without forking a subprocess.
- 'ddx server workers wait <worker-id>' that blocks until the named worker is in a terminal state.
- Document in execute-loop --help that --local is for debugging only; the primary surface is server-worker.

Not worth a separate bead — just ergonomics for the already-correct default.
</operator-feedback>
title: exec: tier-based auto-escalation driven by resource discovery

notes:
Fresh-eyes review completed for power/passthrough model-routing boundary plan.

Reviewed local plan beads and feature/skill docs against upstream agent contract. The architecture direction is right at the highest level: DDx should orchestrate beads, classify success/failure, request abstract power escalation, and persist audit evidence; agent should own harness/provider/model routing and execution. However, the current plan is not yet complete enough to prevent another routing leak.

- ddx-8e9a11c4 critical: DDx plan must align to upstream MinPower/MaxPower contract rather than inventing singular requested Power.
- ddx-52ce6249 high: stale ddx agent run / execute-bead / execute-loop guidance remains in feature and skill docs.

- ddx-b74d4ac6 high: passthrough hard-pin exhaustion needs typed stop semantics; DDx must not widen/remove pins or loop.
- ddx-b0d96cb9 medium: passthrough field ownership needs mechanical allowlist/lint guardrails.
- ddx-8a617e91 medium: catalog/status rendering must stay separate from retry threshold policy.

Primary conclusion: do not start implementation until ddx-c4231775 and its child beads are updated to incorporate these findings. The most urgent correction is replacing all requested-Power language with MinPower/MaxPower request bounds and actual Power evidence, because the upstream agent contract already defines that boundary.

acceptance: File exists at skills/ddx/reference/review.md. Explicitly distinguishes 'bead review' (ddx bead review <id>) from 'quorum review' (ddx agent run --quorum). Documents required evidence format (verdict + per-criterion grade + artifact). Lists at least 3 anti-patterns (hallucinated approval, verdict without evidence, reviewing prompt not diff). No mention of 'context: fork' or .claude/agents/ (those are harness-specific). Linked from skills/ddx/SKILL.md intent-router.
description:
Create skills/ddx/reference/review.md — disambiguates the two 'review' concepts in DDx and guides the harness on which to use.

Content required:
- **Bead review** (ddx bead review <id>): structured grading of a completed bead's implementation against its declared acceptance criteria. Output contract: APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES / BLOCK per AC item, with evidence (file:line references, test output, diff hunks).
- **Quorum review** (ddx agent run --quorum=<policy> --harnesses=<list>): dispatch the review prompt to multiple harnesses and aggregate verdicts. Use for high-stakes changes, controversial refactors, or adversarial checks.
- **Required evidence format** for both: verdict + per-criterion grade + the specific artifact (diff, test output, file path) supporting it. No verdicts without evidence.
- **Anti-patterns**: hallucinated approvals, verdict without evidence, reviewing the prompt instead of the diff.
- **No harness-specific subagent instructions in the skill body.** Quorum review is simply ddx agent run --quorum=<policy> --harnesses=<list> — each harness decides for itself whether to run that in a forked context (Claude: context: fork / .claude/agents/) or inline. Portability over optimization.

Source material: existing /skills/ddx-review/SKILL.md + cli/cmd/bead_review.go for the bead-review output contract. Link to the CLI commands, don't reimplement the grading logic here.

In-scope files:
- skills/ddx/reference/review.md (new)

Out-of-scope:
- skills/ddx/SKILL.md (separate bead)
- Other reference/*.md files (separate beads)
- library/personas/code-reviewer.md (Phase 3)
title: Create skills/ddx/reference/review.md — bead review and quorum review
