acceptance: execute-bead synthesizes a deterministic prompt file from bead data when `--prompt` is omitted; the synthesized prompt includes title, description, acceptance, and available governing metadata instead of only the bead id; execute-loop inherits that contextual prompt automatically; `--prompt` still overrides synthesis; and automated tests fail if the default prompt regresses to the bare `Work on bead <id>.` fallback or if the runner stops receiving the synthesized context.
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: execute-bead default prompt construction. Evidence covers cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go falling back to the literal prompt `Work on bead <id>.`, FEAT-006 requiring execute-bead to resolve the bead and governing artifacts before launch, and a real no-change Codex run in /Users/erik/Projects/agent where the harness received only the bead id and wandered the repo.
</context-digest>
Fix execute-bead so omitted `--prompt` no longer produces an under-specified agent task.

## Required implementation
1. Replace the bare string fallback in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go` with deterministic prompt synthesis driven by the bead record.
2. Build the default prompt from durable bead context:
   - bead id and title
   - description
   - acceptance criteria
   - relevant metadata such as `spec-id`, parent, labels, and any declared governing references when available
3. Materialize that synthesized prompt as a prompt file in the managed execution worktree instead of relying on shell-fragile inline quoting.
4. Keep `--prompt` as an explicit override; only synthesize the default prompt when the caller did not supply one.
5. Make `execute-loop` inherit the same behavior automatically by continuing to call `execute-bead` without needing its own prompt-building fork.
6. Add regression coverage proving the default path no longer sends only `Work on bead <id>.` and that the runner receives the synthesized bead context.

## Boundaries
- Do not invent a new bead schema.
- Do not move prompt assembly into HELIX-specific logic.
- Use DDx-owned bead/store/docgraph context only.

description:
## Scope
Add project-awareness to the MCP tool layer per SD-019 requirements.

## Context
FEAT-002/SD-019 requires ddx_list_projects, ddx_show_project, and project-aware selection semantics for all existing project-local MCP tools. Currently, all 18 MCP tools operate against the single working directory without project scoping.

## Implementation approach
1. Add ddx_list_projects tool — returns project registry entries
2. Add ddx_show_project tool — returns single project detail by ID
3. Add optional "project" parameter to all project-local tools
4. When project param is provided, resolve via GetProjectByID and use that project's path
5. When omitted and exactly one project registered, auto-resolve (singleton compat)
6. When omitted and >1 project registered, return error requesting disambiguation
7. Add MCP-specific tests

## Depends on
ddx-36d2775f (route middleware — shares projectResolver logic)

## Files to touch
- cli/internal/server/mcp.go (or mcp_handler.go) — tool registration + project param
- cli/internal/server/server_test.go or mcp_test.go — MCP project tests
title: Add project-scoped MCP tools: ddx_list_projects, ddx_show_project, and project-aware tool selection

acceptance: A framing design exists for the future service-backed DDx topology, defining central storage/UI vs headless node-gateway responsibilities, git-authoritative vs service-backed data boundaries, degraded local operation when the service is unavailable, and migration from the one-machine multi-project server stage
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: future ddx server control-plane topology. Evidence covers FEAT-002, FEAT-013, architecture.md, and the requirement for a central UI/storage layer with multiple headless servers managing many projects and workers.
</context-digest>
Frame the post-local topology for DDx after the one-machine multi-project server lands.

## Goals
1. Define the boundary between git-authoritative project state and any future service-backed storage/read model
2. Describe a topology with one central storage/control service, one central web UI, and multiple headless `ddx server` node gateways
3. Define what a node gateway owns: project discovery/registration, worker supervision, local execution, and synchronization with the central service
4. Define failure modes, degraded operation, and migration path so local multi-project servers continue to work when the central service is unavailable
5. Keep this as framing only until the one-machine multi-project module is designed and accepted
title: Frame future service-backed multi-node ddx control-plane topology

acceptance:
(1) ClosureGate in cli/internal/bead enforces close invariants ONLY on the automated execution path (execute-loop + Store.CloseWithEvidence): terminal verdict event present (kind=review with APPROVE + non-empty rationale, or kind=review-skipped with reason), execution-evidence event present (base_rev + result_rev). Gate applies to CloseWithEvidence; it does not wrap Store.Close (manual tracker administration remains a separate concern).
(2) Empty-bodied APPROVE verdicts rejected by the gate with a clear error. This is the axon-c5cc071a failure mode.
(3) Table-driven unit test covers at minimum: no execution event, no verdict, malformed verdict, success without result_rev, APPROVE with empty rationale.
(4) Replay of axon-c5cc071a's closure scenario through CloseWithEvidence rejects the close.
description:
Sibling to ddx-738edf47. Codex review of the sequencing plan flagged that the false-closure bug has a broader fix than just its pinpoint: the execute-loop has no invariant check enforcing that every `status: closed` transition carries execution evidence plus a parseable terminal verdict (review APPROVE, review SKIPPED_BY_FLAG, or manual close).

Reporter context: axon bead `axon-c5cc071a` closed in 4m33s with `events: []`, no bundle, no commit — the closure path was walked without any gate.

## Work

1. Identify every call site in cli/internal/agent and cli/internal/server that transitions a bead to `status: closed`. At minimum: execute-loop happy path, reviewer APPROVE path, `--no-review` skip path, manual `ddx bead close`.
2. Centralize closure through a single `ClosureGate` (or similar) that refuses to close unless:
   - execute-bead has a success event referencing a base_rev/result_rev pair, AND
   - a terminal verdict event exists (kind=review with summary APPROVE, or kind=review-skipped with an explicit reason, or kind=manual-close with actor set).
3. Add a table-driven invariant test that constructs synthetic bead states (missing execution evidence, missing verdict, malformed verdict, manual close) and asserts the gate's verdict.
4. Explicit escape hatch for `ddx bead close --force` (manual operator close) — logged with actor + reason, bypasses the gate.

## Relationship to ddx-738edf47

738edf47 is the specific false-closure symptom report + suspected root cause. This bead is the structural invariant test that catches similar regressions regardless of which upstream component (reviewer, execute-loop, worker) walks the close path incorrectly. Both can land together; this one owns the test harness, 738edf47 owns the repro fix.
title: exec: invariant test — no bead close without execution evidence + valid review/skip verdict

acceptance:
1. docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md exists and documents all 10 layers above with file:line citations current as of the document's creation date.

2. The document contains a sequence diagram showing CLI -> NormalizeRouteRequest -> Discovery -> BuildCandidatePlans -> SelectBestCandidate -> Run -> RunAgent -> agent library -> HTTP.

3. The document contains a 'Current Gaps' table with at least the 4 confirmed gaps from epic ddx-2d974641 and links to the tracking beads.

4. FEAT-006 and skills/ddx/reference/agents.md each have a one-line pointer to the new doc.

5. **CI grep verifies citations resolve** (per opus fresh-eyes review). A CI step (or lefthook hook) parses every file:line citation in SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md and verifies the named symbol/line pattern exists in the current source tree. Prevents the doc from rotting silently as routing.go / discovery.go / types.go evolve. Failing citations block the PR.
description:
## Problem

No single document traces the provider/model/harness resolution path from CLI flags to the final HTTP request. SD-015 and SD-023 are SPECS — they describe what should happen in 5 routing modes. The code lives across routing.go, discovery.go, agent_runner.go, runner.go, and agent_cmd.go. A newcomer debugging a routing bug must infer the flow by reading all of them.

The audit for epic ddx-2d974641 turned up a RouteRequest struct missing its Provider field, and a parallel RunOptions.Provider path that bypasses routing — neither of which is documented. This is the kind of hidden structural defect a Resolution Path document would have flagged on first read.

## Fix

Write docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md (or similar location; coordinate with SD-015's author). The document traces one concrete invocation end-to-end:

    ddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6

Walk through every layer, with file:line citations:

1. **CLI parse** — cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go flag extraction (line references).

2. **RouteFlags -> RouteRequest normalization** — routing.go:NormalizeRouteRequest. What each CLI flag maps to; which flags are discarded when they don't match an expected shape; the current gap around --provider.

3. **Discovery probe** — discovery.go:DiscoverProviderModels. When it fires; what it caches; the 30s TTL; how failures are handled.

4. **Candidate planning** — routing.go:BuildCandidatePlans / evaluateCandidate. For each registered harness, the state map lookup and the viability checks.

5. **Fuzzy matching** — discovery.go:FuzzyMatchModel. Pool selection (global vs per-provider), normalization (current gaps), prefix + tiebreak algorithm.

6. **Scoring** — routing.go:scoreCandidate. Per-profile scoring, cost class, historical success rate, provider-affinity bonus (when implemented).

7. **Candidate ranking** — routing.go:RankCandidates / SelectBestCandidate. Non-viable rejection, stable tiebreak.

8. **Dispatch** — runner.go:Run. Harness-specific branches (virtual, agent, script, HTTP-provider-embedded, default exec). For embedded: agent_runner.go:RunAgent -> resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider -> agent library call.

9. **Wire-level request** — ddx-agent builds the HTTP body (system_prompt + messages + tools), sends to the provider. What the server receives.

10. **Response parsing** — streaming or non-streaming decode; where 'unexpected end of JSON input' (ddx-6a5dfe35) surfaces.

Include a SEQUENCE DIAGRAM (mermaid or ASCII) showing the layers.

Include a GAP TABLE at the top listing every currently-missing piece (as of whatever the SD-015 implementation state is) with links to the specific beads tracking each gap.

Keep the document MAINTAINED alongside the code: update it when RouteRequest gains a field, when a new routing mode lands, when fuzzy match gains a normalization step.

## Files likely touched

- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md (new)
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md — add a single-line pointer at the top of the 'Overview' section
- skills/ddx/reference/agents.md — add a one-line pointer in 'Harness / profile / persona dispatch' section so agents consulting the skill can follow up to the full trace when needed

## Out of scope

- Rewriting SD-015 or SD-023 — this doc complements them; they describe intent, this describes current reality.
- Documenting future routing modes that haven't landed.
title: docs: add 'Resolution Path' developer guide tracing CLI flag -> final HTTP dispatch

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/model_catalog_yaml.go (or equivalent) declares an 'omlx' surface with at least the six canonical refs listed above mapping to their server-side IDs on vidar-omlx.

2. Catalog lookup is case-insensitive for the omlx surface (matches per v0.3.11 LookupModelLimits requirement).

3. Unit test in cli/internal/agent/ asserts that for at least 'qwen/qwen3.6' and 'qwen/qwen3.5', catalog.ContextWindowForModel() and the surface lookup return the omlx concrete ID (not the 'embedded-openai' lmstudio-shaped ID).

4. Canonical refs with no omlx target are rejected at route-plan time with a clear diagnostic ('ref X has no omlx target; vidar-omlx offers: [list]'), not a downstream 404 from the server.

5. With the catalog change, 'ddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6' resolves to Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit at the routing layer (the downstream dispatch may still hit the parse bug — fixing that is ddx-6a5dfe35's concern).
description:
## Superseded — wrong diagnosis

Filed under the belief that DDx needs hand-authored omlx surface entries in the model catalog to map qwen/qwen3.6 -> Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit on vidar-omlx.

**This is not the right fix.** An audit of the resolution path (see parent epic ddx-2d974641) showed that the real defect is in fuzzy matching + the RouteRequest plumbing: the fuzzy matcher exists (discovery.go:FuzzyMatchModel) and provider discovery exists (DiscoverProviderModels probes /v1/models on each provider), but:

1. The matcher uses STRICT case-sensitive prefix — qwen/qwen3.6 can't match Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit even semantically.
2. The matcher runs over a provider-union (d.AllModels()) that loses provider identity.
3. --provider is silently discarded at NormalizeRouteRequest (types.go:330 RouteRequest has no Provider field).
4. Catalog-resolved refs (ModelRef path) don't go through the fuzzy matcher at all.

Once those are fixed, no catalog entries are needed — canonical refs fuzzy-match against discovered omlx model IDs at runtime. See sibling beads filed under ddx-2d974641.

Closing as invalid: catalog-first approach would require hand-authoring entries for every new MLX model name format that appears, which defeats the purpose of discovery.
title: catalog: map shipped canonical model refs to omlx-flavor server IDs

description:
src/lib/components/BeadForm.svelte has two issues:

1. Line 130: inputClass uses text-sm — should be text-body-sm (13px/400). Similarly line 131: labelClass uses text-xs — should be text-label-caps (11px/700) since these are uppercase field labels.

2. Lines 162-163: The status <select> applies an inline style:
   style="background-color: var(--status-{status}-surface); border-color: var(--status-{status}-border);"
   These CSS variables (--status-open-surface, --status-open-border, etc.) may not be defined for all status values consistently, and using raw CSS variable injection via a template string is fragile. This sidesteps the Tailwind token system entirely. The dynamic color should be achieved through Tailwind class composition (as done everywhere else in the codebase) rather than inline styles.

3. Cancel/Submit button text uses text-sm — should be text-body-sm.

acceptance: (1) go.mod pins github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.7.0. (2) go -C cli build ./... and go -C cli vet ./... pass. (3) ddx agent run --harness lmstudio --text ping exits 0 against at least one connected lmstudio-type provider. (4) ddx work --once picks a ready bead, routes via profiles, completes without the earlier 'no viable harness' / 'unknown provider' errors.
description:
## Sequencing

Run first within wave:1. All other wave:1 beads (004eccbc fixture capture, 672cf2bf work-loop UX, b9747ee3 registry audit) rebase on top of the v0.7.0 module bump if it breaks the build. Landing this first also lets 004eccbc capture fixtures against current-era reviewer output instead of pinning to v0.6.0-era and filing a follow-up recapture.

---

ddx-agent v0.7.0 is shipping (agent CHANGELOG 2026-04-20). Two consumer-relevant deltas since v0.6.0:

1. **Fixed**: HTTP provider-backed native harnesses (lmstudio, omlx, openrouter, ollama, openai) now dispatch through the concrete provider identity instead of by configured provider name. Unblocks our earlier failure: `agent: native config provider "lmstudio": config: unknown provider "lmstudio"`. (agent-117a0868)

2. **Changed**: `RouteRequest.ProviderPreference` removed from the public surface; profiles (`cheap`, `standard`, `smart`, user-defined) are now the public routing knob. `surface_policy` in the catalog can carry placement order, cost ceilings, failure policy, and reasoning defaults. (agent-285a05d2)

We never wrote any `ProviderPreference` code on the DDx side, so (2) is purely subtractive for us — nothing to remove.

## Work

1. `go -C cli get github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent@v0.7.0 && go -C cli mod tidy`.
2. Verify build: `go -C cli build ./...` — any compile breaks indicate hidden v0.7.0 API shape drift beyond what the changelog flags.
3. Verify no stray `ProviderPreference` references (expect none).
4. Confirm native harness dispatch: `ddx agent run --harness lmstudio --text ping` now succeeds against a connected lmstudio-type provider (bragi in our config).
5. Drain end-to-end: `ddx work` walks ready beads to completion using profiles for routing. Cheap tier should reach local lmstudio; standard/smart can escalate to openrouter.
6. If the catalog's `surface_policy` shape changed, sync `~/.config/agent/models.yaml` to the new schema and document any user-facing config migration in CHANGELOG-of-this-repo.

## Supersedes

- `ddx-4535f466` Track B (ResolveRoute audit to wire `ProviderPreference`) collapses to zero; the architectural argument landed upstream. Keep ddx-4535f466 open for Track A (test-fixture updates) only — those are orthogonal to the v0.7.0 bump.

## Blocks

- `ddx-bbb65768` (profile/automation: local-first default) — depends on v0.7.0's profile-owned placement.
- `ddx-4b59b816` (cost-tiered execution epic) — cheap/standard/smart routing is a precondition.

## Out of scope

- Reviewer malfunctions (ddx-f7ae036f, ddx-f8a11202, ddx-738edf47) — orthogonal to agent version.
- `ddx-672cf2bf` (work-loop silent-exit on epic-only queue) — DDx-local, no agent dependency.
title: ddx-agent v0.7.0 consumer integration: pull, verify, drain the queue

description:
Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_gates_test.go TestExecuteBead_RequiredGateFail_Preserves() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script harness.

Pattern:
- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptHarnessRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.
- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.
- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.
- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.
- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.

Scope limit: ONLY this test. Do not migrate any other test in the same file; touching another test expands blast radius outside the bead.

Verification: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBead_RequiredGateFail_Preserves$' -count=1 passes. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.

Governing concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. Parent: ddx-d9df348d.

acceptance: RealLandingGitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead takes a fromRev parameter. After read-tree HEAD + update-ref, the main worktree reflects the new HEAD for exactly the files changed by the landing commit (no phantom D or M entries), while unrelated local modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat) are preserved. Both Land() fast-path and merge-path call sites pass the correct fromRev. FetchOriginAncestryCheck same. Interface + test stubs updated. go build ./... passes. go test ./internal/agent/... -count=1 passes. Manual verification: after the next worker land, running `git status --short` in the main worktree shows zero phantom entries related to the landing commit.
description:
RealLandingGitOps.SyncIndexToHead in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go only runs `git read-tree HEAD` after update-ref, which syncs the INDEX to HEAD but does NOT update files on disk in the main worktree. Every worker-landed commit that adds or modifies a tracked file leaves the main worktree showing phantom "D" or "M" entries in git status, even though HEAD is correct.

Impact during the Svelte+GraphQL migration epic: opus reviewers have been misled multiple times (reading stale working-tree copies instead of HEAD blobs), and I have manually run `git checkout -- <path>` after nearly every worker close (~6+ times this session). Subsequent agents that read files from the worktree see old content.

Scope of fix:
1. SyncIndexToHead -> SyncWorkTreeToHead. Take a fromRev parameter.
2. After read-tree HEAD, compute `git diff --name-only <fromRev> HEAD` to find the exact files changed by the landing commit
3. Run `git checkout-index -f -- <files>` to restore only those files (preserves unrelated local modifications like agent-logs and beads.jsonl heartbeats)
4. Apply to both call sites in Land() (fast-forward path and merge path) and the FetchOriginAncestryCheck update-ref block
5. Update LandingGitOps interface + test stubs

Alternative considered: git checkout-index -a -f (restore ALL files from index) — rejected because it would clobber legitimate local modifications to files unrelated to the landing commit (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat writes).

This is a P0 blocker for running the migration epic without constant human babysitting. Fix directly via deterministic edit, not via worker dispatch, because the surface is small, the fix is mechanical, and the diagnostic cost of a worker misunderstanding the fix is higher than the implementation cost of just applying it.
title: bug: Land() leaves main worktree stale — update-ref + read-tree doesn't materialize files

acceptance: FEAT-002, FEAT-013, architecture.md, and TP-002 describe one `ddx server` process managing multiple project roots on one machine with explicit project registry, request scoping, per-project isolation, and migration semantics from the current single-project host
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: ddx server topology. Evidence covers FEAT-002, FEAT-013, architecture.md, TP-002, and the current assumption that one `ddx server` serves exactly one repo.
</context-digest>
Design the first-step multi-project `ddx server` module for a single machine.

## Goals
1. Replace the single-project server assumption with a project registry / project-root collection managed by one server process on one machine
2. Define project-scoped API, MCP, and UI routing semantics so clients can enumerate projects and then operate within one selected project context
3. Define per-project config, cache, and adapter boundaries so one bad project cannot poison other served projects
4. Define how project-scoped worktrees and worker pools are managed under one server process without assuming only one active repository
5. Define migration from today's single-project `ddx server` invocation and data adapters without breaking current local use

## Required outputs
- Project registry/discovery contract
- Request routing and project selection model
- Per-project isolation and failure-mode analysis
- Impact on FEAT-008 UI and TP-002 end-to-end coverage
title: Design one-machine multi-project ddx server topology

acceptance:
AC1. Land-coordinator refuses to merge a bead result whose net diff deletes more than N lines from a single file (configurable threshold, default 200) UNLESS the agent's no_changes_rationale.txt or commit message explicitly acknowledges and justifies the deletion (e.g. 'intentional file removal: <reason>').

AC2. Land-coordinator runs a basic sanity gate before merging: the file must still parse for its filetype (TypeScript files compile, Svelte files have valid <script>/<template>/<style> structure if they had one previously, JSON is valid JSON, etc.). Failed parse = preserve as iteration ref, do not merge.

AC3. When a parse-fail or large-deletion gate trips, the bead is marked 'preserved-needs-review' and the operator is alerted via the bead's notes field, with a link to the iteration ref and a one-line summary of what gate fired.

AC4. Post-merge typecheck/build is run as a final gate (project-level command from .ddx/config.yaml or sensible default like 'cargo check' / 'bun run build'). If post-merge build fails, AUTOMATICALLY revert the merge and preserve the result instead. This is the safety net for cases AC2 doesn't catch.

AC5. Documentation in docs/ explains the safety gates, how to override them when intentional (e.g. for genuine large deletions), and what 'preserved-needs-review' means operationally.

AC6. A regression test on the harness validates that an attempt to merge a result deleting >N lines (or producing a parse failure) is correctly preserved+rejected, not merged.
description:
Today on axon repo, the ddx-land-coordinator merged a bead-result branch onto master that deleted 1448 lines from one Svelte component (1552 → 104 lines). The same merge also corrupted a second test file (1043 → 158 lines, overwritten with unrelated content). Master became unbuildable: vite-plugin-svelte threw 'Expected token }' because the policies/+page.svelte file lost its <script lang="ts"> wrapper along with the entire HTML template and style sections.

The damaging merge:

  commit be1073f4333c289e48680cf8c05ceefa7ea9c075
  Merge: 0c5557b 2f35ad2
  Author: ddx-land-coordinator <coordinator@ddx.local>
  Date:   Wed Apr 29 20:56:46 2026 -0400

      Merge bead axon-80979cb8 attempt 20260430T005434- into master

       .../databases/[database]/policies/+page.svelte     | 1448 --------------------
       1 file changed, 1448 deletions(-)

The post-merge codex reviewer DID block subsequent attempts that built on the broken base ('the diff only adds execution metadata' / 'the new Playwright coverage is incorrect... the collection view never loads'), but the original 1448-line deletion went through unchecked. Five subsequent worker attempts ran on top of the now-broken master, each adding ~80 lines of partial reconstructions, none producing a valid Svelte file.

Net damage on master before manual recovery:
  ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte      1552 → 186 lines (1366 lines lost)
  ui/tests/e2e/policy-enforcement.spec.ts      1043 → 158 lines (885 lines lost)
  master state: vite-plugin-svelte fails to compile, UI bundle cannot build, docker e2e harness cannot start

Manual recovery (commit bc4b492 on axon master): git checkout HEAD~14 of both files, re-stage, commit, push. 2415 lines restored across 3 files.

Why this happened (hypothesis):
  1. The bead-attempt branch had a partial reconstruction of the file (104 lines of just imports/state, no template).
  2. The land-coordinator did a 3-way merge with master at base_rev. The base_rev was ALSO the partial reconstruction (because EARLIER attempts had already truncated it).
  3. Three-way merge produced a 'clean' delete of the 1448 lines that existed only on master's side because base_rev was already broken.

Or alternatively: the agent's tool calls deleted content from the worktree without re-emitting the full file (e.g. used Edit with overlapping replace blocks that effectively truncated), the harness committed whatever was on disk without sanity-checking magnitude, and the land-coordinator merged the partial content as authoritative.
title: execute-bead: land-coordinator merged a result that deleted 1448 lines from a single file (catastrophic damage)

Loads /nodes/:nodeId/agents. Asserts: (1) all three workers appear in the table, (2) each row has a non-empty 'Project' column matching the seeded project name, (3) row order is by started-at descending, (4) clicking a row navigates to /nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/agents/:workerId with the URL params matching the clicked worker's project and id, (5) when a worker's state transitions from running to exited the table updates within one refresh cycle. Backend Go test asserts GET /api/agent/workers returns workers from multiple projects in a single response array (already true today; lock it in).
description:
When an operator has N projects in flight (e.g. ddx, axon, helix) the FEAT-021 routing puts each project's workers under /nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/agents. To see what's running across ALL projects you currently have to click into each project separately. Add a node-level (combined) view that lists every running worker on the node regardless of project, so the operator has one situational-awareness pane.

Backing data: GET /api/agent/workers already returns ALL workers managed by the server (it's a node-wide endpoint, not scoped to a single project). The UI just needs to render this list with a project column.

UI requirements:
- Route: /nodes/:nodeId/agents (combined; sibling of /nodes/:nodeId/beads and /nodes/:nodeId/sessions)
- Columns: project name, worker id, bead id, harness, model, state, started-at, attempts, last-status
- Auto-refresh every ~3s
- Each row links to /nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/agents/:workerId for the per-worker detail (which is ddx-7be82986)
- Project picker dropdown should also expose 'All projects' as a value that navigates to this page
- Empty-state message when zero workers are running
title: Dashboard UI: cross-project workers view (all live workers across all projects on one screen)

description:
Per codex review: DDx routing_signal_tmux.go (with ReadClaudeQuotaViaTmux, ReadCodexQuotaViaTmux, ANSI strip, output parsers) is duplicated by agent internal/harnesses/{claude,codex}/quota_tmux.go (commit 8099293). DDx should not keep parallel tmux scrape parsers long-term.

Two paths:
A. Agent owns tmux scrape; DDx triggers refresh via service.HealthCheck(harness target). The agent does the tmux invocation internally; DDx never shells out tmux. Delete DDx routing_signal_tmux.go.
B. Remove the DDx-side doctor refresh path entirely. Quota state populates lazily via the agents normal probe cadence; doctor --routing reads from cache only.

Recommendation: A. service.HealthCheck(harness) already triggers a fresh probe internally (per the implementation report from agent-3afb76bb). The tmux refresh becomes implicit when DDx asks for HealthCheck on claude/codex. No new contract surface needed.

After this decision: routing_signal_tmux.go can delete from DDx (folded into the file-delete cascade); doctor --routing migration uses service.HealthCheck instead of explicit RefreshXQuotaViaTmuxForWorkDir.
notes:
REVISED per user: quota awareness is non-negotiable, agent owns the harness execution surface, so quota probing implicitly moves to ddx-agent.

REVISED DECISION:
- DDx routing_signal_tmux.go DELETES (per ddx-70c48576). Duplicate of agent capability.
- AGENT retains internal/harnesses/{claude,codex}/quota_tmux.go as the canonical tmux scrape.
- DDx delegates quota refresh to agent via service.HealthCheck (after agent-side enhancement) OR via the cached HarnessInfo.Quota field that ListHarnesses already populates.
- AGENT-SIDE follow-up bead filed for: extend HealthCheck or ListHarnesses for claude/codex to refresh tmux quota on demand. Until that lands, DDx doctor reads cached values (populated by the agents normal probe cadence; may be stale but never breaks).
- Future: agent could add direct PTY-based quota probing as an alternative to tmux. Not blocking.

CONSEQUENCES:
- ddx-fc2527c5 (doctor --routing migration): calls service.ListHarnesses; quota in HarnessInfo.Quota; if stale, calls service.HealthCheck to trigger refresh (after agent-side enhancement lands).
- ddx-70c48576: DDx routing_signal_tmux.go + test deletes (agent canonical version stays).
- New agent-side bead: extend HealthCheck for tmux harnesses to refresh quota.
title: Decision: tmux quota refresh ownership — agent owns it; remove DDx routing_signal_tmux.go duplicate

acceptance: FEAT-006, FEAT-002, and FEAT-008 define a structured embedded-agent progress contract for managed runs; the contract names the required phases and fields, distinguishes runtime-only progress from tracked execution evidence, and defines one shared read model for CLI and server/UI consumers
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: embedded ddx-agent runtime observability and live worker progress. Evidence covers FEAT-006 execution/evidence ownership, FEAT-002 server activity surfaces, FEAT-008 web UI expectations, execute-loop worker supervision, and the need for DDx-owned progress telemetry beyond coarse session logs when DDx runs the embedded harness.
</context-digest>
Define the progress-event contract for managed embedded-agent runs so DDx can surface meaningful live execution status in the CLI and server/UI.

## Goals
- Define structured phase/heartbeat output for embedded-agent runs (queueing, launch, running, post-checks, landing, preserved, done)
- Define the minimal per-event fields needed for operator visibility and later reporting: worker/project/bead/run identity, phase, elapsed/heartbeat timing, harness/provider/model/profile, and token/cost deltas when available
- Clarify which live progress artifacts remain runtime-only and which summaries/indexes are exposed through DDx-owned APIs and UI surfaces
- Keep provider-native transcript ownership external; DDx progress output should be normalized runtime telemetry, not transcript duplication

## Required spec work
- Update FEAT-006, FEAT-002, and FEAT-008 so embedded-agent progress visibility is an explicit contract rather than an implementation detail
- Clarify how progress events relate to tracked execution artifacts, session logs, and server-managed worker state

## Required implementation planning
- Define the server/API/UI read model for live worker progress and recent phase history
- Define how CLI and UI consume the same structured progress feed without introducing hidden workflow policy
title: Add structured embedded-agent progress output for workers and UI

acceptance:
AC1. `ddx agent workers prune` (or equivalent) exists and reaps registry entries whose recorded PID is no longer alive (or whose started_at is older than a configurable threshold). Output lists each reaped worker with id, bead_id, harness, age.

AC2. `ddx agent workers stop --bead <id>` succeeds against a stale registry entry too — it should release the registry slot even when the underlying process has already exited. Current 'no matching workers' / 'worker not running' contradiction must be resolved.

AC3. The local-worker registry persists or is reconstructed in a way that survives ddx-server restarts cleanly: either persisted with PID liveness check on restart, or rebuilt from execute-bead/execute-loop on-disk evidence (.ddx/executions/<id>/result.json).

AC4. `ddx agent workers --json` includes a 'liveness' or 'pid_alive' field per entry so operators can see at a glance which entries are live vs stale, even before pruning.
description:
When a local execute-bead/execute-loop process exits abnormally (killed, OOM, parent shell terminated, container crash) the worker entry can remain in the in-memory registry forever. `ddx agent workers` lists it as state=running with elapsed_ms continuously advancing, even though no PID exists.

Reproducing today (axon repo): worker local-20260429T030828-075a28c5 (bead hx-5f4d4ea1, harness=agent, model=qwen3.6) started at 03:08 UTC, has logged elapsed=10+ hours, and `ps -ef | grep execute-bead` shows nothing. The git worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-hx-5f4d4ea1-* has only the checkpoint commit (no actual work commits), confirming the worker died early.

Available paths to clean up are all unsatisfying:
1. `ddx agent workers stop --bead hx-5f4d4ea1` returns 'no matching workers' (because the matcher only finds live workers)
2. `ddx agent workers stop <worker-id>` returns server error 400 'worker not running' (same contradiction — the registry says running, the stopper says not running)
3. `systemctl --user restart ddx-server` works but kills every other active worker on the box, including ones for other repos that are doing real work
4. Manual edit of server-state.json risks corruption and isn't documented

The bead itself wasn't blocked (status=open, owner=null) so the registry noise is mostly cosmetic — it pollutes `ddx agent workers` output and confuses operators trying to triage the queue. But over weeks of use these accumulate (saw 14+ stale entries from prior sessions on this same box, with ages 134h–268h).
title: agent workers: stale 'running' entries can't be cleaned without server restart

Removed/deprecated website command pages point users to `ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx work`, or compare-prompts as appropriate.\n3. `git diff -- website/content/docs/cli/commands docs/dev docs/migrations` shows only docs wording/classification changes.

acceptance: (1) A regression test at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go (or execute_bead_land_test.go) runs a simulated successful execute-bead attempt against a real temp git repo and asserts: (a) after the run returns, the working tree is clean (git status --porcelain is empty), and (b) at least one file under .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ is present in git log --all --oneline for the attempt's result or successor commit. Test must fail on current HEAD (98e379b) and pass post-fix. (2) The same test uses bead tracker evidence to confirm the bead's closing_commit_sha still resolves after the merge (git cat-file -e). (3) cli/internal/server/workers_test.go TestWorkerManagerStartAndShow (or a sibling test) exercises the server-worker path and asserts the same clean-worktree guarantee. (4) Full test suite (cli: go test ./cmd ./internal/agent ./internal/server) passes. (5) Manual dogfooding: ddx agent execute-loop --once against a ready bead leaves git status --porcelain empty, and HEAD or HEAD^ contains at least one file under .ddx/executions/<attempt>/. (6) No changes to AGENTS.md, FEAT-006, or skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md — the policy is already correct; this is implementation only.
description:
## Scope

execute-bead's orchestrator currently leaves the per-attempt evidence files it
materializes (`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`,
`result.json`, `checks.json`, `usage.json`) untracked after the agent run
returns. Successful bead commits reach main via the "Merge bead" path, but the
evidence sits in the working tree unstaged and orphaned. This bead closes that
gap: after the agent finishes, DDx (not the agent) stages the evidence and
returns the working tree clean.

Concrete evidence of the gap: commit 98e379b on main is a one-shot backfill
that imports 254 historical `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/` directories
(2026-04-11 → 2026-04-15) that were never staged by the execute-bead pipeline.
That backfill must not need to happen again.

## Files to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` — the land/merge flow. After the
  agent's commit is produced (or after `Merge()` / bare-ref update), and
  before returning `ExecuteBeadReport`, stage the per-attempt evidence dir and
  fold it into the merge. Two shapes are acceptable:
  (a) amend the evidence into the bead's own commit (agent's commit + DDx's
      evidence files, single commit), OR
  (b) emit a trailing `chore: add execution evidence [<attempt-id>]` commit
      on the working branch as a sibling to the bead's commit, in the same
      merge transaction.
  Choose (b) if amending the agent's commit would rewrite its SHA — the agent
  commit's SHA is referenced by `session_id` / `closing_commit_sha` and must
  not move. Choose (a) only if the evidence can be staged BEFORE the agent
  commits (e.g. the agent is told via `git add` hooks to pick up a DDx-seeded
  index). Pragmatic recommendation: (b).
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — the per-run orchestrator entry. After
  the executor returns, verify the working tree is clean; if it is not,
  diagnose untracked files introduced by this run, stage them into the
  trailing evidence commit (if they are under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/`),
  and fail the run with a clear error otherwise ("orchestrator left worktree
  dirty: <path>").
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — the server-worker path (`runWorker`,
  `buildAgentRunner`). Same contract applies: after `ExecuteBead` returns and
  `LandBeadResult` is called, the worker must verify the worktree is clean
  before declaring the attempt complete. If the land flow is still dropping
  evidence, surface that as a test failure, not a silent leak.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` OR a new
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go` — regression tests.

## Files NOT to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 875-960 — the per-bead
  instructions text (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` /
  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`). Those are agent-facing and must keep
  telling the agent to `git add <specific-paths>` only. The fix belongs in
  the orchestrator, not the prompt: DDx wrote the evidence, DDx commits it.
- `AGENTS.md`, `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md`,
  `skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md` — the policy is already correct as of commit
  3a2a0c5. This bead implements the policy; it does not amend it.

## Cross-Bead Context

- Parent epic `ddx-32b3008e` ("Evolve execute-bead into a tracked
  execution-evidence workflow", P0, spec-id FEAT-006) is the meta for this
  work. This bead is the first execution-layer task under that epic.
- The trail-preservation policy is codified in commit 3a2a0c5: AGENTS.md
  "Merge Policy" section, FEAT-006 "Execution Trail Preservation in
  Downstream Merges" section (with 3 acceptance criteria), and
  skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md "Branch Merge Policy" section.
- The land flow today uses a mix of `git merge --no-ff` and
  `git update-ref` (for bare-repo worktrees) — see
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:166-544 for the three code paths
  that touch the merge. Any of them may be the right insertion point; pick
  the one that runs LAST after the agent commit is finalized.
- The bug-fix commit 2792dfc ("anchor SessionLogDir to project root") gives
  the orchestrator a reliable absolute path for session logs. This bead is
  the analogous fix for execution evidence.

## Spec Excerpt (FEAT-006 Execution Trail Preservation, normative)

> **Execution Trail Preservation in Downstream Merges** [...]
> Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git
> history; routing, cost, latency, retry-count, and tier-escalation reports
> read these commits directly. The trail realizes the `output = bead(input)`
> contract the system is built on: every bead attempt is recoverable from
> git history alone.
>
> **Acceptance Criteria (execution trail preservation):**
> - Given a branch containing at least one `chore: update tracker
>   (execute-bead` commit, when the branch is merged to its target, then
>   every commit SHA on the feature branch remains reachable from the target
>   branch HEAD after the merge.
> - Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when the branch containing
>   that commit is merged to main, then `git cat-file -e
>   <closing_commit_sha>` still succeeds on main after the merge.

This bead adds a fourth, implementation-layer criterion: that the
per-attempt evidence dir is *present in the trail at all*, not merely
preserved across merges.

## Verification Commands

- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestExecuteBeadLandsEvidence`
  passes (new regression test, see AC below).
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd` passes
  (full suite; no regressions).
- Manual dogfooding: in a throwaway clone, run
  `ddx agent execute-loop --once` against a ready bead. After it returns,
  `git status --porcelain` MUST print nothing. `git log -1 --stat` MUST show
  at least one file under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` in the HEAD commit
  (or its immediate predecessor in the same merge).
title: orchestrator: land execution evidence and leave worktree clean after agent run

acceptance:
(1) Reviewer verdict extraction consumes NORMALIZED reviewer text (the harness's final cleaned response), not raw stream frames. If the agent module does not yet expose a normalized final-response API, this bead is blocked on the agent-side stream normalizer bead (sibling in ~/Projects/agent).
(2) Fixture reviewer-stream-approve-misextracted.jsonl (from ddx-004eccbc) drives a regression test: the pre-fix behavior records BLOCK; the fix records APPROVE.
(3) ParseReviewVerdict no longer defaults to BLOCK on an unrecognized verdict line. Unrecognized output returns a parse error; the caller handles that as a review-error (feeds ddx-738edf47's retryable-failure path), not a silent BLOCK.
(4) Unit tests cover: clean APPROVE, clean BLOCK, REQUEST_CHANGES, unparseable, empty.
description:
## Wave 2 framing: risk-reduction, not correctness

This bead (scope:partial) stops silent-BLOCK mis-records — it does NOT stop mis-extraction itself. After wave:2 lands, the reviewer will still misfire on codex/claude raw stream frames because clause (1) (consume normalized final text) is deferred to ddx-7bc0c8d5. What changes: misfires surface as loud review-error reopens instead of silent BLOCKs, feeding ddx-738edf47's retryable-failure path. Expect a "review-error reopens per closed bead" metric to look rough in this window. The real extraction fix lands when agent-32e8ff5e ships upstream and ddx-7bc0c8d5 migrates.

---

Reporter: nexiq+axon 2026-04-18/19/20. Symptom 1 of review-malfunction report.

The post-merge reviewer in `ddx agent execute-loop` records `{"verdict":"BLOCK"}` in `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/result.json` even when the reviewer LLM produced a well-formed `### Verdict: APPROVE` (or REQUEST_CHANGES) with full AC grade table and cited evidence. Observed ~20 times in a row on nexiq codex+claude loop 2026-04-19 against beads nexiq-b5661a77, nexiq-50485cd3, nexiq-be1bc61d, nexiq-8999acab, hx-8a26c6df, hx-4901d83a, hx-e33210f6, nexiq-35d64a6b, and more — every bead in the run.

Code site: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:76` — `reVerdictLine` regex `^#{1,4}\s+Verdict:\s*(APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|BLOCK)\s*$`. When the reviewer output contains the raw verdict on a heading line but is wrapped inside a codex streaming JSON event body (e.g. `{"type":"item.completed","item":{..."text":"### Verdict: APPROVE..."}}` with escaped newlines), the regex does not match across the wrapped payload and `ParseReviewVerdict` falls through to the `VerdictBlock` default at line 86 ("safe default: requires human attention"). The bead then gets a `review_malfunction` event with detail "post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)".

Actual reviewer output is preserved in `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` under the reviewer session id and can be recovered post-facto; the parser just fails to see it.

Hypothesis from report: parser anchors on `### Verdict:` heading but a codex harness stream serializes text with `\n` escaping or wraps the reviewer body inside thread/turn JSON events so the multiline anchor never hits. Root fix options: (a) extract reviewer text from the raw stream before parsing (strip codex thread/turn JSON to the inner `item_0.text` payload); (b) switch the reviewer prompt to require machine-readable JSON `{verdict, ac_grades, summary}` and parse that; (c) broaden the regex to match non-anchored `### Verdict: X` headings even inside JSON-escaped text.

## Upstream dependency

Blocked on agent-32e8ff5e (in ~/Projects/agent): the fix moves reviewer-text extraction behind the ddx-agent service boundary so DDx no longer parses raw harness stream frames. Until that lands, f7ae036f's clause (1) on "normalized reviewer text" cannot be satisfied and the regression test from ddx-004eccbc remains red. The parse-error / no-default-BLOCK changes (clauses 3-4) can land independently and are worth doing now.

## Scope split (wave 2)

Clauses (3) and (4) — no default-BLOCK, parse errors surface as review-error, fixture regression test — land in wave 2 with only ddx-004eccbc as a prereq.

Clause (1) — consume normalized final text from the agent service instead of raw stream frames — is deferred to ddx-7bc0c8d5 because it depends on agent-32e8ff5e shipping upstream. This bead is labeled scope:partial to signal that wave 2 only closes (3)+(4); a follow-up pass on (1) happens inside ddx-7bc0c8d5's migration.
title: exec: post-merge reviewer mis-extracts verdict, emits spurious BLOCK on APPROVE

acceptance:
1. Per-field hard cap of 65,535 bytes on bead.events[].body and adjacent writer paths, documented in cli/internal/bead package comment. bd-compatibility rationale in the comment.
2. Reviewer raw streams never enter bead.events[].body. Reviewer output path writes the full stream to .ddx/executions/<id>/reviewer-stream.log; event body is ≤512 bytes (verdict + first-line rationale + path reference). Unit test: a 2MB reviewer stream produces a <=512-byte event body referencing the full payload on disk.
3. Other writers that append to bead.events[].body enforce the per-field cap; overflow persists as an artifact under .ddx/executions/<id>/ and the event body carries a path reference plus summary.
4. Reader: bead/session scanner buffers raised from the current 1MB to at least 16MB (covers observed extremes: 1.46MB bead row, ~7MB session rows). No new global "shared buffer constant" abstraction — raise each call site's existing Buffer() arg (cli/internal/bead/store.go:172, processmetrics.go:723, session_log_tailer.go:91). claude_stream.go:101 is already 16MB.
5. Regression test: a synthetic beads.jsonl line of 2MB reads cleanly; a line exceeding the buffer surfaces an error naming the bead id and does not crash.
6. bd round-trip smoke: a DDx-authored bead with one 65,535-byte description field exports to JSONL and is accepted by `bd import`. Requires bd on PATH; otherwise t.Skip() with a clear message.
7. Migration / recovery: corrupted rows encountered during read fall back to best-effort parse (truncated body + warning); do NOT mutate existing tracker files in-place. A separate `ddx bead repair` bead can be filed if real corruption is found in the wild.
8. cd cli && go test ./... green.
description:
Reporter: nexiq+axon 2026-04-18/19/20. Symptom 2 of review-malfunction report.

A reviewer emitted a ~1.46MB event body (streamed reviewer output dumped verbatim into bead.events[].body). Because cli/internal/bead/store.go:172 sets bufio.Scanner.Buffer to 1MB, subsequent reads of beads.jsonl fail with "bufio.Scanner: token too long." Tracker effectively bricked until the row is hand-trimmed.

## Compatibility constraint (validated 2026-04-20)

Empirical bd test (/tmp/bd-size-test via homebrew bd 1.0.2):
- Per-field insert cap: 65,535 bytes. Exactly Dolt TEXT column limit. 65535 inserts; 65536 errors "string too long."
- Per-line export: easily exceeds 1MB when a bead accumulates multiple long comments/events. Observed: one bead with 5 × 65KB comments → 326KB line. Our 1.46MB corruption is ~23 such events, plausible for a long-running bead.
- bd export lines ARE currently unreadable by DDx when they exceed our 1MB scanner cap — even when no single field is oversized.

## Design contract

- Write-side (DDx): enforce per-field hard cap of 65,535 bytes so DDx-authored beads round-trip through `bd import`. Soft cap ~10KB with head+tail+marker truncation for typical event bodies. Overflow persists as an artifact file under .ddx/executions/<id>/, event body stores path + one-line summary.
- Read-side (DDx): accept arbitrarily long lines from bd-compatible tools. Scanner buffer sized generously (suggest 64MB) so normal accumulated beads never trigger token-too-long.
- Read-side tolerance: if a line does exceed the buffer, surface a clear error with bead id and pointer to the artifact, do not crash.

## Known scanner sites

All currently undersized relative to bd export reality:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:172 (1MB)
- cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:723 (1MB)
- cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:101 (16MB — probably OK)
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_tailer.go:91 (4MB)

## Artifact reference

Fixtures for this bug (2MB synthetic reviewer stream, 1.46MB-style beads.jsonl row) land via ddx-004eccbc.
title: exec: reviewer stream bloats bead event body, corrupts beads.jsonl via bufio.Scanner limit

acceptance: skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl exists with at least 15 rows, each valid JSON, each with phrase + expected_reference + expected_cli keys. scripts/eval-skill.sh exists and is executable. make eval-skill runs in under 10 minutes against both --harness claude and --harness codex and exits 0 when the SKILL.md routing matches the fixtures. make eval-skill exits non-zero if any row fails. scripts/eval-skill.sh --validate passes against the current SKILL.md (frontmatter has only name + description; no Claude-only fields; body under 500 lines; all referenced reference/*.md files exist). CI runs make eval-skill on PRs that modify skills/ddx/**.
description:
Anthropic's skill-authoring guidance treats evaluations as load-bearing, not optional. Ship a cross-harness eval suite for the ddx skill so CI can enforce routing behavior on every PR.

Components required:
1. skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl — JSONL file with at least 15 rows, each a user phrase + expected reference file + expected CLI invocation. Cover every intent-router entry in SKILL.md plus edge phrasings. Example rows:
     {"phrase":"what is on the queue", "expected_reference":"reference/status.md", "expected_cli":"ddx bead ready"}
     {"phrase":"drain the queue", "expected_reference":"reference/work.md", "expected_cli":"ddx work"}
     {"phrase":"run the next bead", "expected_reference":"reference/work.md", "expected_cli":"ddx work"}
     {"phrase":"got any work for me", "expected_reference":"reference/work.md", "expected_cli":"ddx bead ready"}
     {"phrase":"review this", "expected_reference":"reference/review.md", "expected_cli":"ddx bead review"}
     ... 10+ more covering beads, agents, status, doctor.
2. scripts/eval-skill.sh — driver that for each row:
     a. Runs 'ddx agent run --harness <h> --text <phrase>' against --harness claude and --harness codex.
     b. Parses the harness output and checks whether it mentions the expected_reference filename or invokes the expected_cli command (regex or grep-based check is fine for v1; tighten later).
     c. Reports pass/fail per row per harness. Exits non-zero on any fail.
   Also supports a --validate flag that does agentskills.io spec conformance on skills/ddx/SKILL.md (no Claude-only frontmatter fields, body < 500 lines, all referenced reference/*.md files exist, name and description present).
3. Makefile target 'eval-skill' (in cli/Makefile or root Makefile) that runs scripts/eval-skill.sh. Add 'make eval-skill' to CI on PRs that touch skills/ddx/.
4. CI config (.github/workflows/*.yml or equivalent) — add a step that runs make eval-skill when skills/ddx/** changes.

In-scope files:
- skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl (new)
- scripts/eval-skill.sh (new)
- Makefile (new or edited target)
- .github/workflows/ci.yml or equivalent (add step)

Out-of-scope:
- The agent eval framework itself — v1 is a simple bash driver; a more sophisticated harness can come later.
- SKILL.md content (separate bead); eval suite assumes SKILL.md exists.

The eval suite replaces the older manual 'ask Claude and Codex a few questions in a scratch dir' smoke test with CI-enforceable checks.
title: Ship ddx skill eval suite (routing.jsonl + eval-skill.sh + CI)

acceptance:
1. ddx work --model qwen3.6 discovers qwen3.6-35b-a3b via prefix match and routes there.
2. When a provider has both qwen3.6 and qwen3.6-35b-a3b, exact match wins (selects qwen3.6).
3. Shortest suffix wins: qwen3.6-27b (suffix len 4) beats qwen3.6-35b-a3b (suffix len 8).
4. On equal suffix length, catalog entry preferred over discovered-only.
5. Fuzzy match prints: model "qwen3.6" resolved to "qwen3.6-27b" (fuzzy match).
6. Deprecated model prints warning but proceeds.

Unit tests:
T1. Prefix match: qwen3.6 → qwen3.6-35b-a3b when only prefix available.
T2. Exact beats prefix: qwen3.6 → qwen3.6 when both exist.
T3. Shortest suffix tiebreak: qwen3.6-27b wins over qwen3.6-35b-a3b.
T4. Catalog preferred over discovered on equal suffix length.
T5. Tie warning emitted when multiple candidates survive all tiebreakers.

cd cli && go test ./... && go build .
description:
Add fuzzy prefix matching to model resolution so --model qwen3.6 resolves
to qwen3.6-35b-a3b when a provider advertises it.

Depends on ddx-71fe194f (discovery integration) being landed first — this
bead extends the unified model pool with prefix matching.

Implementation:
- In the model resolution step, after checking for exact match, check for
  prefix matches across the unified pool (catalog + discovered).
- Tiebreak: shortest suffix after prefix. On equal suffix length, prefer
  catalog entries over discovered-only, then alphabetical.
- When fuzzy match is used, print: model "X" resolved to "Y" (fuzzy match).
- When resolved model has deprecation metadata, print warning.
- When multiple models tie after all tiebreakers, warn and pick first.
execute-loop-last-detail: agent: provider error: received error while streaming: {"message":"Error rendering prompt with jinja template: \"Unknown StringValue filter: safe\".\n\nThis is usually an issue with the model's prompt template. If you are using a popular model, you can try to search the model under lmstudio-community, which will have fixed prompt templates. If you cannot find one, you are welcome to post this issue to our discord or issue tracker on GitHub. Alternatively, if you know how to write jinja templates, you can override the prompt template in My Models > model settings > Prompt Template."}
title: Fuzzy model resolution: prefix match with shortest-suffix tiebreak

acceptance: 1. New ExecuteBeadStatus value or extra field captures 'declined: needs decomposition' as a structured outcome (not regex on rationale). 2. Loop policy: a bead that has produced 'declined: needs decomposition' is auto-set to status=blocked (or equivalent terminal-for-loop status) and is no longer in the dep-ready execution queue. 3. The recommended sub-beads from the decomposition rationale are captured as a structured event on the bead (kind:decomposition-recommendation), not just inline text. 4. CLI: a first-class command for inspecting and clearing execute-loop cooldowns (e.g. ddx bead cooldown show / clear). The current --set/--unset workflow becomes a power-user fallback. 5. Regression test: a fake executor that returns 'declined: needs decomposition' is invoked via the loop; assert (a) bead transitions to blocked after the first declination, (b) no second attempt happens, (c) decomposition recommendation is on the bead as a structured event. 6. Migration: documented in DDx CHANGELOG; existing 'execute-loop-retry-after' key continues to work as a power-user override but is deprecated for the decomposition case.
description:
Today, when execute-bead returns no_changes with rationale = 'this epic is too big, split it into sub-beads', the loop sets a short execute-loop-retry-after (a few hours) and keeps re-attempting the epic forever. The bead surface gives operators no good knob: 'unset retry-after' lets it re-burn, 'set retry-after = 30 days' is arbitrary, and there is no signal that the bead is structurally not a single-pass deliverable.

Observed in this session on ddx-fdd3ea36 (the routing point-release epic): two consecutive drain attempts produced identical no_changes recommendations to split into 9 sub-beads. The agent burned ~$1 of claude tokens on the second attempt re-deriving the same conclusion.

What is needed:

1. A first-class outcome from execute-bead: 'declined: needs decomposition' (distinct from no_changes/execution_failed). Carries the recommendation in a structured field, not buried in the no-changes-rationale string.

2. When the loop sees 'declined: needs decomposition', it should auto-set the bead to status=blocked (or a new status like needs-decomposition), record the recommended sub-beads as a bead-comment / event, and STOP re-attempting until a human or helix-evolve splits it.

3. Or: detect the loop — if the same bead has produced the same no_changes rationale N times in a row (e.g. 2), auto-mark it blocked with reason 'agent declined repeatedly; needs decomposition'.

4. Make 'execute-loop-retry-after' inspectable / settable via a first-class CLI flag rather than --set / --unset on a magic key. Document the field. The 'unset retry-after' shortcut should NOT exist — operators should clear it via 'ddx bead clear-cooldown' or similar.
title: execute-loop: epics that decline with 'decompose me' should mark themselves blocked, not rely on ad-hoc cooldowns

description:
## Context

The frontend Playwright suite under `cli/internal/server/frontend` currently starts `bun run preview` on port 4173, which serves static SvelteKit output without the Go DDx backend. The parent e2e suite now includes direct API tests and route/page tests that require real DDx server data. TC-008 in `e2e/app.spec.ts` calls `/api/health`, `/api/documents`, `/api/beads`, `/api/beads/status`, `/api/personas`, and `/api/docs/graph`; those endpoints return 404 under static preview but return 200 from the Go server.

Build a fixture-backed default Playwright harness that boots the Go DDx server on a deterministic local port and points Playwright at it. The fixture must be self-contained so e2e runs do not read the developer's home config or the repository's live `.ddx/` state.

## In-scope files

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.config.ts` — change the default `webServer` to run the Go DDx server (`go run ./cli` from the repo root or a built `ddx` binary) on a deterministic port, set `use.baseURL` to that server, and keep any static-only preview coverage separate from the default backend-backed project.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/` — add a fixture project directory containing minimal `.ddx/beads.jsonl` data with open, closed, ready, and blocked beads; a tiny `docs/` library; and any config needed by the server.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/app.spec.ts` — only TC-008 API-test alignment if the fixture data requires narrow expectation updates.

## Out-of-scope

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.demo.config.ts`.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts`.
- Go server source under `cli/internal/server/`; the API is assumed correct.
- Frontend components or routes.
- Migrating TC-001 through TC-007 page tests; those are separate child beads.

## Rollback / cleanup

Do not leave a half-populated fixture directory. Do not make tests pass by skipping or deleting them. The default test harness must use a temp copy or otherwise isolated fixture workspace, not the developer's actual repository checkout or `$HOME`.
title: Switch the default Playwright e2e harness to a Go-server fixture

description:
Parent: ddx-efe058be. Implement the first narrow safety gate from the Axon truncation incident. Before Land advances the target branch, inspect the diff from BaseRev to ResultRev and reject/preserve results that delete more than a configured number of lines from any single file unless the worker commit message or landing rationale explicitly acknowledges the large deletion.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go
- config type/defaults only if needed for the threshold

Out-of-scope:
- Filetype syntax parsing
- Post-merge build rollback
- UI/server worker reporting
- Documentation beyond code comments/help text needed for the gate

Default threshold: 200 deleted lines per file. Override/acknowledgement should be explicit text such as intentional file removal or intentional large deletion in the worker commit message or result rationale.
title: land safety: reject unacknowledged large deletions before target ref update

acceptance:
**User story:** After the upstream point release lands and DDx pulls it, `ddx agent run` with no flags just works on my machine. `--profile local`, `--harness claude`, `--model opus-4.7` behave per the upstream contract. The drain-queue failure mode (empty spec → gemini+minimax → 19-burn) is impossible.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Upstream prerequisite tagged and pulled.** Go.mod references a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class `default` and `local` profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs + gating; `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible`; public decision-trace (`RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error); cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` with unknown-cost fallback. All upstream beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` labeled `routing-plan` are closed.

2. **No DDx-side synthesis on default path.**
   - `ddx agent run` with zero flags issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call, no Model, no Harness, no Provider.
   - `ddx work` default: same shape.
   - `ddx agent execute-bead` with no overrides: same shape.
   - Integration test: same config that caused the drain-queue failure (real `~/.local/share/ddx/.ddx/config.yaml`) now either succeeds or returns a single typed error — never 19-burn.
   - Unit test: `workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker({})` → spec with `Profile: "default"`, no other fields. No `ResolveTierModelRef`, no `ResolveProfileLadder` in the call graph on the default path.

3. **Preflight before claim.**
   - Call sequence at `execute_bead_loop.go:352`: next-candidate → `ResolveRoute` → (preflight gate, new) → `Claim` → invoke. Route error OR preflight rejection exits the loop with a `worker-level` failure — no bead claimed.
   - Test: seed a pathological route (harness with allow-list that excludes the decision's model — fabricated via upstream test seam). Assert `beadStore.Claim` is never called; worker record shows `status: execution_failed` with detail naming the rejected pair; no bead has a `kind:tier-attempt` event from this attempt.

4. **Config deprecation is deliberate, not warning-only.**
   - Loading a config with `agent.routing.default_harness` set produces a **hard error** at startup with a migration pointer. Field is deleted from the config schema.
   - Loading a config with `agent.routing.profile_ladders` or `agent.routing.model_overrides` set produces a one-time warning per process AND causes the default path to ignore them. Opt-in with `--escalate` or `--override-model` explicit flags.
   - CHANGELOG entry + migration guide in DDx docs.
   - Migration test: fixture config containing all three deprecated fields, run `ddx agent run` with no flags, assert warnings printed and default path honored (not the deprecated config).

5. **Override behavior matches G3 exactly.**
   - `--harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. If claude fails upstream's gates (binary missing, quota exhausted), surfaces the typed error; no silent substitution. E2E test mocks each gate failure.
   - `--model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Lowest-cost available quota-OK provider wins. E2E test seeds 3 providers at different cost with shared model; asserts lowest-cost pick.
   - `--harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` when combo is invalid; clean dispatch otherwise.
   - `--profile local --harness claude` → upstream typed error ("claude is non-local, profile=local is local-only"); DDx surfaces it unmodified. E2E test.

6. **Playwright — drain-queue goldens.**
   - Fixture matches user's real config (endpoints: lmstudio + omlx + lmstudio + lmstudio, no gemini binary test or with gemini installed but not in allow-list for the chosen model).
   - Click Drain on project home → within 5s: worker row appears with harness = `agent` (or another live-endpoint-backed harness). Never gemini + non-gemini model. Never 19 failures.
   - After drain completes: worker status `success` (or typed error exit with reason recorded on the bead). Attempts count matches actual work done.

7. **No compensating DDx logic retained.**
   - Search DDx for code that re-implements any of: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping, provider cost scoring. Must be absent. If found, PR fails review.
   - `ResolveProfileLadder`, `ResolveTierModelRef`, `workersByHarness` escalation helpers are deleted from the default path. They remain reachable via `--escalate` only. Dead-code detector run in CI catches orphans.

8. **D6 / D7 are explicitly out of scope and filed as follow-ups.**
   - `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter) remains its own bead and may land before or after this one.
   - A new bead is filed post-release for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer work, pointing at A4 upstream as satisfied.

9. **Documentation.**
   - DDx's CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md links to upstream profile catalog + override precedence + best-provider contract docs. No re-documentation on the DDx side.
   - DDx CHANGELOG entry covers the go.mod bump, D4 breaking changes, and new CLI surface semantics.

10. **Regression goldens for the drain-queue bug.**
    - Integration test exists that reproduces the original failure mode (empty spec + user's config + gemini binary installed + minimax model in overrides). Before this bead's work: test fails with 19-burn. After: test succeeds or exits with one clean error. Test stays in the suite as a regression guard.
description:
## Status after codex review (2026-04-22)

Original plan was consolidated in this bead but **reduced in scope** after codex's review surfaced that most of the compatibility and data-model work belongs upstream in ddx-agent. This bead is now the **DDx-side tracker only**. Upstream work is queued as separate beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` (prefix `agent-*`) and will land as a single tagged point release; nothing in DDx lands until that release is pulled.

## Goals (unchanged — user stated 2026-04-22)

**G1. Zero-interaction defaults.** `ddx agent run` and `ddx agent execute-bead` just work with no flags.

**G2. Usable profiles.** `--profile standard`, `--profile local` force clear, documented rules.

**G3. Usable overrides.** `--harness claude` forces claude. `--model opus-4.7` forces opus-4.7. Within requirements: available, quota OK, lowest cost.

## Upstream dependencies (tracked in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/)

This bead blocks on a single upstream point release that carries:

- Profile catalog with first-class `default`, `local`, `standard` (and any others DDx needs to expose) + documented provider-preference rules.
- Supported-models allow-list on routing-inputs `HarnessEntry`; gating rejects non-allowed models even when `ExactPinSupport: true`.
- Typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` surfaced from `Execute` so explicit `--harness X --model Y` conflicts fail fast with actionable detail.
- Public `RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error for "no viable candidate" with per-candidate rejection reasons — DDx's decision-trace consumers need this.
- Cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` (`CostUSDPer1kTokens`) + scoring tiebreak that prefers lower cost, with unknown-cost fallback, subscription-quota pricing, and near-quota demotion.
- Documentation for the profile catalog, override precedence, and "best provider" contract.

Once that release is tagged, DDx work below proceeds.

## DDx-side changes (reduced scope)

**D1. Dispatch sends a real spec.**

`workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker` treats empty input as `{Profile: "default"}` explicitly. Dashboard Drain button calls the same path. No DDx-side default synthesis beyond "profile=default if unset."

**D2. Stop synthesizing a tier ladder by default.**

Default `ddx agent run` / `ddx agent execute-bead` / `ddx work` path calls `agentlib.ResolveRoute(Profile: spec.Profile)` with no Model string unless `spec.Model` was explicitly given. No per-tier iteration, no `ResolveTierModelRef` lookup, no preference-order fallback. The tier-ladder machinery becomes opt-in via `--escalate` only.

**D3. Preflight gate (defensive only, post-upstream).**

After `ResolveRoute` returns, DDx verifies the decision's `(Harness, Model)` is not in the typed-incompatibility set surfaced by upstream. The compatibility data comes from upstream — DDx does **not** duplicate the allow-list. Insertion point: `execute_bead_loop.go:352`, **before** `Claim`. Route/preflight failure exits the loop with a worker-level failure record; no bead is claimed, no burn cycle.

**D4. Deprecate DDx-side routing config.**

Deliberate breaking change in the same release that pulls the upstream point release:
- `agent.routing.default_harness` — field deleted; upstream's profile model makes it redundant.
- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` — kept but gated behind explicit `--escalate` only. Not read on the default path. Warning at config-load if set without `--escalate` ever being used in the life of the process.
- `agent.routing.model_overrides` — same treatment as `profile_ladders`: explicit-opt-in only, warning otherwise.
- `agent.harness` (top-level) — kept, clarified as a **preference for tie-breaks**, not a default override.

Migration note published in DDx changelog.

**D5. CLI surface parity with goals.**

- `ddx agent run` with zero flags → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")`. No other DDx inputs.
- `ddx agent run --profile local` → pass through unchanged to upstream.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. Upstream picks best provider within claude.
- `ddx agent run --model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Upstream picks lowest-cost available quota-OK provider.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` from upstream when incompatible; clean success otherwise.
- `ddx work` (drain) default → same as `ddx agent run` default.

## Explicitly dropped from this bead (per codex)

- **D6 — `ddx agent route-status` / providers UI decision-trace consumers.** Adjacent but not required. Files as a follow-up once A4 lands upstream. Hooks into `ddx-23978824` (providers UI).
- **D7 — `workersByProject` id/path filter.** Independent bug. Stays as `ddx-05b4cc9d`; not blocked by this bead and does not block this bead.

## Sequencing

1. **Upstream beads land and point release is tagged.** (Separate queue in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.)
2. DDx `go.mod` bumps to that release.
3. D1, D2, D3, D5 land together in one DDx PR — no compensating paths retained.
4. D4 lands with D1-D3-D5 or in the immediate follow-up PR. Migration warning + changelog.
5. **Then** D6 / `ddx-23978824` / sessions-billing-mode (`ddx-6904a90b`) work can consume the new decision-trace surface.

## Cross-references

- Upstream beads: see `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` under label `routing-plan`.
- Direct codex review feedback logged in bead notes below.
- Supersedes original 3-step proposal from conversation history.
- Addresses `project_endpoint_routing_design.md` memory directive (2026-04-21): "kill named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match."
- Hard downstream dependents (wait for this bead):
  - `ddx-b6cf025c` — drain-worker count control (default profile must mean something first)
  - `ddx-23978824` — providers UI (needs decision trace from A4)
  - `ddx-6904a90b` — sessions billing-mode (benefits from richer session.routing metadata)
- Independent of this bead: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter).

## Codex review — key corrections folded in

1. A3 confirmed by code inspection: `HarnessEntry` has no model-allow-list field; `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery; `gating.go:88` only checks `ExactPinSupport`. Allow-list must be added to routing inputs/capabilities, not just "fix ExactPin semantics."
2. A4 bigger than originally written: public `RouteDecision` has no `Candidates` field; service drops rejection data on error. Needs typed error + public surface.
3. Earlier claim that codex/claude have `Models: nil` was wrong — that's DDx's local `harness_config.go`, not upstream's `internal/harnesses/registry.go`. Upstream has codex=[gpt-5.4], claude=[sonnet, opus, claude-sonnet-4-6]. Allow-list work is universal upstream.
4. Upstream catalog has no `default` or `local` profile today (models.yaml:195). Must be added before DDx materializes them.
5. D3 restricted to defensive-only; all compatibility logic belongs upstream.
6. G2 precedence conflict (`--profile local --harness claude`): resolved in upstream docs as explicit-pin-wins but upstream returns typed error if the pin violates the profile's hard constraint (e.g., local-only with non-local harness).
7. G3 cost needs explicit data model — no price field on ProviderEntry today.
8. D4 reframed as deliberate breaking change, not warning-only drift.
9. D6 + D7 dropped from epic scope.
10. Quick-test gates added: upstream exact-pin regression; service-layer typed error preserves reasons; `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` against endpoint-only config; DDx worker exits before any bead claim.
notes:
SUPERSEDED by ddx-c4231775 after fresh-eyes review findings.

This epic encoded the older plan where DDx called ResolveRoute in execution paths, accepted default/local profiles, preserved harness/model override semantics, and kept preflight/candidate-trace behavior in the work path. That plan conflicts with the current boundary decision: DDx only estimates effort and chooses cheap/standard/smart profile; agent owns all harness/provider/model routing; ResolveRoute is status/debug-only.

Do not execute this epic or use its AC as implementation guidance. Open review findings and the live symptom bead have been re-parented under ddx-c4231775.
title: Adopt ddx-agent routing point release: zero-interaction defaults, usable profiles, sharp overrides (DDx-side consumer)

- [ ] Template documented (required sections, expected output contract)
- [ ] Reads governing-doc refs from bead fields
- [ ] Used by the post-review path from gap #1
description:
Manual review prompt construction is tedious and inconsistent. A command that reads a bead and produces a review-ready prompt (AC + commit diff + governing-doc excerpts + expected output format) would make post-merge review cheap enough to always run.

## Scope
- ddx bead review <bead-id> [--iter N] [--output <file>]
- Pulls bead AC from the bead record
- Pulls commit diff via git show (latest successful execute-bead result_rev by default, or --from-rev)
- Reads governing-doc slugs from bead 'spec-id' / 'refs' fields and excerpts the relevant sections
- Renders a prompt template asking the agent to grade each AC item and produce structured APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES / BLOCK
- Output is the prompt body (stdout or to a file); pipe into ddx agent run --prompt @-

## Context
Observed during axon ADR-018 loop: I wrote /tmp/review-A1.md by hand twice (iter 1 and iter 2). Both prompts had the same structure. A templated command would eliminate that manual step and make reviews reproducible.
title: exec: ddx bead review <id> — template a review prompt from bead + commit
